section.section: Inc-07 (Inpatient & Nursing) — PREREQUISITE: Cross-module seams (Spring Modulith named interfaces, ports/DTOs, allowed-edge list)
section.overview:
**Sub-increment: prerequisite for 07a/07b/07c** — the cross-module wiring that must land before any inpatient domain/application code, mirroring the inc-08 `clinical::api` / `billing::api` reuse pattern. The `inpatient` module is today a bare stub (`backend/src/main/java/com/otapp/hmis/inpatient/package-info.java` — `@ApplicationModule, Stub for increment 00`, no domain/application/api). This slice declares the module's `allowedDependencies`, the ports/DTOs it consumes, and the ONE new published clinical seam it requires — all so `ApplicationModules.verify()` stays green (no cycles).

**Five seams, with decisions:**

**(1) `AdmissionStatus` typed enum — OWNED by inpatient, no cross-module leak.** A typed `inpatient.domain.AdmissionStatus` mapped 1:1 to the legacy free-text strings via an `@Converter` (ADR-0014 §1 typed-enum-to-legacy-string pattern, same as `PrescriptionStatus`). Exactly five values: `PENDING("PENDING"), IN_PROCESS("IN-PROCESS"), STOPPED("STOPPED"), HELD("HELD"), SIGNED_OUT("SIGNED-OUT")` — verbatim from `Admission.java:45` (free-text String) and the corrected lifecycle in the ground-truth model. **NO `TRANSFERRED`** (Q8 — WardTransfer is a confirmed phantom, parked CR-07-ward-transfer). The DB column persists the exact legacy strings (including the hyphenated `IN-PROCESS`/`SIGNED-OUT`) so 3 years of data migrate without value transformation. This enum stays inside `inpatient.domain` — it never crosses a module boundary, so no `@NamedInterface` is needed for it.

**(2) New published clinical seam for `PatientPrescriptionChart` — WRITE owned in `clinical`, called via a published port (`inpatient -> clinical::api`).** The entity lives in `clinical.domain` (V27, `admission_uid` loose ref, package-private repo, NO api surface today). **Decision: the chart write is owned by the CLINICAL module, not inpatient**, and inpatient drives it through a new published `clinical::api.PrescriptionChartPort`. Rationale: (a) the chart has REAL intra-module `@ManyToOne` FKs to `Prescription`, `Consultation`, `NonConsultation` (`PatientPrescriptionChart.java:57-105`) — moving the write to inpatient would force those FKs to become loose refs or create an illegal `inpatient -> clinical.domain` reach-through; (b) the 07b dosing-note guards key on the linked `Prescription` being GIVEN (`PatientServiceImpl.java:2544`) which is a clinical-owned status; (c) it mirrors the inc-08 precedent exactly — `pharmacy` orchestrates dispense but `clinical` owns the `Prescription` flip via `PrescriptionDispensePort`. So inpatient passes `admissionUid` + dosing fields into `clinical::api`, which performs the write inside the caller's tx. The admission-IN-PROCESS gate (`PatientServiceImpl.java:2564-2577`) is evaluated in inpatient (it owns admission status) BEFORE the call; the GIVEN-prescription + nurse-uid + exactly-one-encounter guards are enforced in the clinical write port (it owns the prescription + the V27 CHECK). Edge: `inpatient -> clinical::api` (one-directional; `clinical` has NO edge to `inpatient`).

**(3) Billing discharge gate + payment-driven activation — REUSE existing `BillingQueries.getBillStatus` (live recompute) + NEW inpatient settlement listener.** *Discharge bills-cleared gate:* **recommend the legacy-faithful live recompute** over a new `billsOutstanding(admissionUid)` query. The legacy gate (`PatientResource.java:5342-5357` discharge / `:5593-5603` referral / `:5851-5882` deceased) iterates `PatientInvoice -> PatientInvoiceDetail -> PatientBill.getStatus()` and rejects if ANY bill is `UNPAID` **or** `VERIFIED`. There is NO `billsCleared` field. Reproduce this by iterating the admission's bill uids and calling the EXISTING `BillingQueries.getBillStatus(billUid)` per bill — no new billing API surface, no denormalised flag, byte-identical predicate. A new `billsOutstanding` aggregate query is parked as an optional optimisation (not freezable now; not needed for parity). *Payment-driven activation:* `ConsultationSettlementListener` (`clinical`) silently ignores ward/registration bills (confirmed `ConsultationSettlementListener.java:59-64`), so inpatient MUST add its OWN `inpatient.application.AdmissionSettlementListener` consuming `shared.event.BillSettledEvent` with `@TransactionalEventListener(phase = BEFORE_COMMIT)` — same pattern as `PatientClosureListener`/`ConsultationSettlementListener`. On a ward-bed bill PAID it promotes the patient's PENDING admission(s) -> IN-PROCESS + bed WAITING -> OCCUPIED, reproducing `PatientBillResource.java:352-365`. The event lives in `shared.event` (OPEN module), so this adds NO new compile edge and NO cycle.

**(4) Deceased-finalize publishes the EXISTING `PatientDeceasedEvent` from the inpatient service in-tx (Q6).** The inpatient deceased-finalize service publishes `shared.event.PatientDeceasedEvent(patientUid, actorUsername)` via `ApplicationEventPublisher` inside its own transaction; the existing `registration.PatientClosureListener` (`:84-102`) flips `Patient.type = DECEASED`. NO new event, NO new listener, NO new field, and critically **NO `inpatient -> registration` compile edge** (the event type is in `shared.event`). Inpatient does NOT call clinical `ClosureService` (it is OPD-only). This is distinct from the OPD path that publishes the same event from `clinical`.

**(5) Q13 consumable pricing routes via existing `billing::api.recordClinicalCharge(kind=MEDICINE)` — bill-line literals FLAGGED as CR-blocked.** The consumable charge reuses `BillingCommands.recordClinicalCharge(ChargeRequest, ctx)` with `kind = ServiceKind.MEDICINE` and `serviceUid = medicineUid`, so `PriceLookup.resolve(plan, MEDICINE, medicineUid)` reproduces cash = `Medicine.price` + the `MedicineInsurancePlan` covered path — no schema change, no new ServiceKind. **OWNER-GATED:** engagement-lead must confirm Q13=MEDICINE (frozen pending owner confirm). The MEDICINE pricing path itself is freezable once confirmed; but `ChargeRequest` carries NO `billItem`/`description` fields (`ChargeRequest.java:27-37`), so reproducing the legacy bill-line literals `billItem='Medication'` / `description='Consumable: <name>'` is display drift requiring **CR-07-Q13-billing-display** — those literal-display criteria are `frozen:false`.

**Allowed-dependency edge list for `inpatient` (`@ApplicationModule(allowedDependencies=...)`):**
`{ "shared", "clinical :: api", "billing :: api", "masterdata :: lookup", "iam :: lookup" }`
- `shared` — `AuditableEntity`, `TxAuditContext`, `BusinessDayService`, `AuditRecorder` (ADR-0007), `shared.event.{BillSettledEvent, PatientDeceasedEvent}`, `ErrorCode`, `GlobalExceptionHandler`.
- `clinical :: api` — the NEW `PrescriptionChartPort` (write+read) + existing `PrescriptionReadPort`/`ConsultationLookup` as needed.
- `billing :: api` — `BillingCommands.recordClinicalCharge`/`cancelCharge`, `BillingQueries.getBillStatus`, `ChargeRequest`/`ChargeResult`, `SettlementPolicy`, `PaymentMode`.
- `masterdata :: lookup` — `Ward`/`WardType`/`WardBed`/`WardCategory` (V6), `ServiceKind.WARD`/`MEDICINE`, `ProcedureType`.
- `iam :: lookup` — nurse/clinician personnel existence.

**No reverse edges:** `clinical`, `billing`, `registration`, `pharmacy`, `masterdata`, `iam` gain NO dependency on `inpatient`. The only new edge clinical-side is the new `PrescriptionChartPort` published in the EXISTING `clinical::api` named interface (no new module exposed). `ApplicationModules.verify()` + ArchUnit stay green.

**New `@NamedInterface` declarations:** NONE required from `inpatient` for THIS prereq slice (inpatient is a leaf consumer here; whether it later publishes an `inpatient::api` for reporting is out of scope). One new TYPE is added to the EXISTING `clinical :: api` named interface: `PrescriptionChartPort` + its DTOs (`PrescriptionChartView`, `RecordPrescriptionChartCommand`) — declared in `com.otapp.hmis.clinical.api` so they inherit the existing `@NamedInterface("api")` on that package.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: GIVEN the inpatient module package-info WHEN it declares @ApplicationModule THEN allowedDependencies is exactly {"shared", "clinical :: api", "billing :: api", "masterdata :: lookup", "iam :: lookup"} and ApplicationModules.verify() passes with no cycle (no module gains a dependency on inpatient).

check.section: Inc-07 (Inpatient & Nursing) — PREREQUISITE: Cross-module seams (Spring Modulith named interfaces, ports/DTOs, allowed-edge list) [sub-increment: prereq for 07a/07b/07c]
check.reason: The section smuggles NO deviation into the frozen baseline and applies every charter gate correctly — that part is clean. I verified against the legacy source and the as-built code: SEAM-02 excludes TRANSFERRED (no 6th state); SEAM-03/04 reproduce the free-text PatientPrescriptionChart dosing-note path with NO MAR/route/dispensedAt and keep the verbatim "Admission not verified" reject (legacy PatientServiceImpl.java:2544/2549-2562/2571-2572 confirmed); SEAM-07 reuses the EXISTING PatientDeceasedEvent/PatientClosureListener with no new event/listener/field/code and no inpatient->registration edge (PatientClosureListener.java:84-102 confirmed); SEAM-08 is billing-only via recordClinicalCharge(kind=MEDICINE), correctly frozen:false on owner-confirm Q13 (the allowed-edge list correctly omits pharmacy/inventory — no stock decrement/FEFO/last-unit-409); SEAM-09 correctly marks the legacy literals billItem='Medication'/description='Consumable: <name>' (verbatim-confirmed at PatientServiceImpl.java:2312-2313) frozen:false on CR-07-Q13-billing-display; no STALE_ENTITY/409/pessimistic-lock, no DischargePlan.kind, no SoD/SELF_APPROVAL_FORBIDDEN, no admit-time deceased guard, no active-flag honouring, no AdmissionBed-close-at-discharge, no Q11-bug fixes, no ExternalMedicalProvider FK, no Envers/device-fingerprint, no *-WRITE/*-DISCHARGE codes, no 403-on-wrong-role parity assertion, no ADMISSION sequence, no scheduler-as-frozen (CR-07-Q2 correctly parks it). Sub-increment labelling (prereq before 07a/07b/07c) is correct. HOWEVER, two frozen:true ACs are NOT buildable as written against the actual as-built seams and would produce SILENT BEHAVIOUR DRIFT on safety-critical financial/lifecycle gates — which under my mandate is blocking. (1) AC-INC07-SEAM-05's discharge bills-cleared gate cannot be reproduced by inpatient "iterating the admission's bill uids" because NO admission-scoped billing query exists (billing queries are patientUid-scoped only; legacy scans patientInvoiceRepository.findAllByAdmission at PatientResource.java:5342) and inpatient cannot enumerate the bills attached to the admission by OTHER modules (lab/radiology/pharmacy) — so the gate as drafted would MISS those bills and wrongly pass discharge; the draft compounds this by explicitly mis-parking the admission-scoped enumeration as "not needed for parity." (2) AC-INC07-SEAM-06's AdmissionSettlementListener cannot resolve "the patient's PENDING admissions" or "a ward-bed bill" because shared.event.BillSettledEvent carries ONLY (billUid, settledAt) — no patientUid, no bill-kind — and SettlementDispatcher publishes it only on CASH-bill PAID; the legacy promotion (PatientBillResource.java:352-365) keys on the bill's PATIENT (not on bill-kind), so the draft's "on a ward-bed bill PAID" trigger is itself a mis-statement of the legacy trigger and the seam is non-functional against the existing event shape. These are seam-mechanics/parity-fidelity defects in frozen:true ACs, not smuggled deviations; they must be corrected before this prereq can be relied upon.
corrections: AC-INC07-SEAM-05 (BLOCKING — behaviour drift on a safety-critical financial gate): the gate cannot be made legacy-faithful by 'iterating the admission's bill uids' from inpatient. Legacy scans patientInvoiceRepository.findAllByAdmission(adm) -> PatientInvoiceDetail -> PatientBill.getStatus() (PatientResource.java:5342-5352), catching EVERY bill linked to the admission's invoices — including lab/radiology/procedure/pharmacy bills inpatient never created and whose uids it does not hold. PatientInvoice is billing-domain-owned and there is NO published admission-scoped billing query (BillingQueriesImpl exposes only getBillStatus(billUid) + worklistAdmits; all other billing reads are patientUid-scoped). Correct the AC to REQUIRE a new published billing seam (e.g. BillingQueries.admissionHasOutstandingBills(admissionUid) or billStatusesForAdmission(admissionUid)) that reproduces the findAllByAdmission scan inside billing where PatientInvoice is owned, and re-classify it as IN-BASELINE-REQUIRED for parity — NOT 'an optional optimisation, not needed for parity' as blockedItems #3 currently states. Also note getBillStatus is documented (BillingQueries.java:32-40) as scoped to the add_report bill-gate ONLY ('settlement gates ... MUST NOT use this'); reusing it for the discharge gate violates its own published scope contract and is the wrong seam regardless.

AC-INC07-SEAM-06 (BLOCKING — non-functional against the existing event; and a mis-stated legacy trigger): shared.event.BillSettledEvent is record(String billUid, Instant settledAt) ONLY — it carries no patientUid and no bill-kind, and SettlementDispatcher.java:69-71 publishes it solely on CASH-bill PAID. The listener as drafted cannot (a) resolve the patient to find PENDING admissions, nor (b) determine the bill is a ward bill. Correct the AC to specify the resolution mechanism explicitly: either (i) the listener stores/matches the admission's ward-bed billUid (the uid returned by recordClinicalCharge at admit) so it promotes when THAT specific bill settles, or (ii) a billing seam resolves billUid->patientUid. Additionally fix the trigger description: legacy PatientBillResource.java:352-365 promotes ALL of the bill's patient's PENDING admissions on ANY of that patient's bills being paid in that approval path — it does NOT filter on bill-kind='ward'. State the trigger as legacy does (patient-scoped on bill payment), not 'on a ward-bed bill PAID'. Confirm whether the existing CASH-only BillSettledEvent publication path even fires for the ward-bed activation case, or whether a new publication point is needed (escalate to solution-architect if the event must be extended — that is a seam change, not a free reuse).

AC-INC07-SEAM-04 cite precision (ADVISORY): the 'Admission not verified' reject for the prescription/consumable chart path is at PatientServiceImpl.java:2571-2572 (verified), and IN-PROCESS continue at 2573-2574. Confirm that the additionally-cited :2062-2069 is the correct second charting location (vitals/nursing) rather than a stale line ref; align the cite to the exact method the dosing-note path uses.

AC-INC07-SEAM-03/04 ownership split (ADVISORY — confirm, do not smuggle): legacy enforces ALL guards (GIVEN @2544, exactly-one-encounter @2549-2562, admission-status @2571-2577) in ONE method. Splitting the admission-status gate into inpatient and the GIVEN/encounter guards into the clinical port is a modern boundary arrangement (allowable), but the AC must assert the guard SEMANTICS and EXACT legacy messages ('Prescription not picked from pharmacy' @2545, 'Admission not verified' @2572, the exactly-one-encounter messages @2550-2562) are reproduced byte-identically across the boundary so no message/behaviour drift hides in the seam.

Bill-status enum mapping (ADVISORY): SEAM-05 asserts the gate rejects on UNPAID or VERIFIED and that COVERED bills do NOT block — verified against PatientResource.java:5347. Ensure the new admission-scoped billing query maps the legacy String statuses to billing.domain.BillStatus 1:1 (UNPAID/VERIFIED block; PAID/COVERED pass) so the predicate stays byte-identical; do not let the typed-enum translation silently reclassify any status.

section.section: 07a — Two-phase admission lifecycle + admit guards + payment-driven activation
section.overview:
## Inc-07a: Two-phase admission lifecycle, admit guards, payment-driven activation, and all three disposition terminals

**Sub-increment:** 07a (the first of three slices: 07a admission+discharge+ward, 07b nursing-charts, 07c consumable+accrual)

**Module:** `com.otapp.hmis.inpatient` — currently an empty stub (`package-info.java` only). Every entity, repository, service, and controller listed below must be built from scratch.

### What this slice builds (exact-process baseline)

**Entities (new Flyway migrations V44+):**
- `Admission` — ULID uid + hidden BIGINT id per ADR-0014 §1; `status` as typed `AdmissionStatus` enum mapped 1:1 to legacy strings `PENDING / IN-PROCESS / STOPPED / HELD / SIGNED-OUT` (no `TRANSFERRED`); loose `patientUid` VARCHAR(26) cross-module ref (no FK); loose `wardBedUid` VARCHAR(26) updatable=**true** (legacy `Admission.java:62-65` is `updatable=true`; DDL must NOT mark it non-updatable — see 03-DECISIONS-RATIFIED.md ADR/doc corrections); `paymentType`, `insurancePlanUid`, `membershipNo`; `admittedAt`, `dischargedAt` (nullable — deceased path never stamps dischargedAt); `@Version` optimistic lock inherited from `AuditableEntity`; no `billsCleared` flag (recomputed live); no `businessDayUid` on Admission.
- `AdmissionBed` — per-period bed-occupancy/billing ledger; `status` CHECK-constrained `OPENED / CLOSED`; `openedAt TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL`; `closedAt TIMESTAMPTZ NULL` (NULL on terminal admissions — legacy leak, CR-07-Q10); mandatory `@OneToOne PatientBill` (loose `patientBillUid`); loose `wardBedUid`, `admissionUid`, `patientUid` (non-updatable FKs matching legacy `updatable=false` on AdmissionBed.java:40-68).
- `DischargePlan` — flat entity; narrative TEXT columns (`history/investigation/management/operationNote/icuAdmissionNote/generalRecommendation`); `DispositionStatus` enum `PENDING/APPROVED`; `approvedBy/approvedOn/approvedAt` populated by copy from creator at finalize; loose `admissionUid`.
- Inpatient `ReferralPlan` (admission path) — **reuses the existing `referral_plans` table / `clinical.domain.ReferralPlan` entity** via the `admissionUid` loose ref already pre-wired in V28. The inpatient referral endpoints write the existing entity with `admissionUid` set and `consultationUid` null. No second table is created.
- `DeceasedNote` — `patientSummary` TEXT NOT NULL; `causeOfDeath` TEXT NOT NULL; user-entered `date DATE` + `time TIME` (legacy date/time split, NOT a server timestamp); `DispositionStatus` enum `PENDING/APPROVED`; loose `admissionUid`.

**Core behavioural rules reproduced verbatim:**

1. **`doAdmission` two-phase flow** (`PatientServiceImpl.java:1701-2021`): admit guard ORDER matters — (a) patient-not-found 404, (b) existing PENDING/IN-PROCESS admission rejects 422 with verbatim message `"Could not process admission. The patient is already admitted"` (`PatientResource.java:5194-5200`), (c) bed-not-found 404, (d) bed not active rejects 422 `"Could not process admission, bed not active"` (`PatientServiceImpl.java:1703-1704`), (e) bed not EMPTY rejects 422 `"Could not process admission, bed already occupied. Please select a different bed."` (`:1707-1709`). On success: bed→WAITING, new `Admission(PENDING)`, new `Visit(PENDING, sequence=SUBSEQUENT-FOR-ADMISSION)`, UNPAID `PatientBill(billItem='Bed', description='Ward Bed / Room', qty=1, amount=WardType.price)` (`PatientServiceImpl.java:1753-1760`), new `AdmissionBed(OPENED, openedAt=now, patientBillUid=wardBedBill.uid)`, `patient.type=INPATIENT`.

2. **Insurance path + top-up split** (`PatientServiceImpl.java:1795-1966`): selection loop `findByInsurancePlanAndCovered(plan, true)` — NO active-flag filter (CR-07-Q9 parked); max-price-OR-exact-plan-match (`:1802-1808`). If `eligiblePlan != null`: mutate wardBedBill to `COVERED` at `eligiblePlan.price`, create PENDING insurance invoice + detail (`:1811-1871`). Top-up branch (`:1880`): when `eligiblePlan.insurancePlan.id != patient.insurancePlan.id AND (WardType.price - eligiblePlan.price) > 0`: create supplementary `PatientBill(status=UNPAID, billItem='Bed', description='Ward Bed / Room (Top up)')` linked via `principalBill` / `supplementaryBill` self-refs + own PENDING cash invoice + detail (`:1881-1948`); admission stays PENDING / bed stays WAITING. No-top-up `else` branch (`:1950-1963`): sign out PENDING/IN-PROCESS consultations, set `admission=IN-PROCESS`, `wardBed=OCCUPIED` immediately.

3. **Payment-driven activation** (`PatientBillResource.java:352-365`): when a ward-bed bill is paid, the `AdmissionSettlementListener` (new inpatient listener consuming `BillSettledEvent` BEFORE_COMMIT) finds all PENDING admissions for the patient, promotes each to IN-PROCESS + bed to OCCUPIED, and signs out IN-PROCESS consultations. This listener lives in `inpatient` and imports only `shared.event` (no inpatient→clinical compile edge). The existing `ConsultationSettlementListener` is NOT modified.

4. **Charting gate** (`PatientServiceImpl.java:2062-2069`): admission PENDING → 422 `"Could not be done. Admission not verified"` (any charting operation); IN-PROCESS → allowed; else → 422 `"Could not be done. Patient already signed off"`.

5. **Worklists as filters** (`PatientResource.java:3610-3633`): doctor-inpatient-list = `{IN-PROCESS, STOPPED}`; nurse-inpatient-list = `{IN-PROCESS}`. PENDING admissions appear in neither.

6. **Discharge terminal** (`PatientResource.java:5259-5289`, `5342-5385`): `saveDischargePlan` sets `admission=STOPPED` + creates `DischargePlan(PENDING)`. `getDischargeSummary`: bills-cleared gate (scan ALL admission PatientBills; reject 422 `"Could not get discharge summary. Patient have uncleared bills."` if any UNPAID or VERIFIED — `:5342-5352`); approve all invoices; if status=STOPPED: `admission=SIGNED-OUT`, `wardBed=EMPTY`, `patient.type=OUTPATIENT`, `patient.paymentType=CASH`, `patient.insurancePlanUid=null`; stamp `dischargedBy/On/At` from plan.createdBy/On/At; `DischargePlan=APPROVED`. `approvedBy=createdBy` copy (`:5378-5381`). AdmissionBed left OPENED (legacy leak, CR-07-Q10).

7. **Referral terminal** (`PatientResource.java:5446-5459`, `5610-5632`): `saveReferralPlan` sets `admission=STOPPED`, `ReferralPlan(PENDING)`. `getReferralSummary`: same bills-cleared gate; approve invoices; if STOPPED: `admission=SIGNED-OUT`, `wardBed=EMPTY`, `patient.type=OUTPATIENT`, resets; `ReferralPlan=APPROVED`. Provider field: mandatory `externalMedicalProviderUid` NOT NULL + `@NotBlank`, no FK (mirrors V28). AdmissionBed left OPENED.

8. **Deceased terminal** (`PatientResource.java:5719-5732`, `5889-5908`): `saveDeceasedNote` validates `patientSummary + causeOfDeath` NOT NULL; if `admission.status == null` defensively sets IN-PROCESS; if IN-PROCESS: `admission=HELD`, `wardBed=EMPTY` (bed freed early at HELD). `getDeceasedSummary`: bills-cleared gate; approve invoices; if HELD: `admission=SIGNED-OUT`, `wardBed=EMPTY`, patient.type=DECEASED (via `PatientDeceasedEvent` published in-tx), `DeceasedNote=APPROVED`. `approvedBy=createdBy` copy. `dischargedAt` NOT stamped on deceased path. `PatientDeceasedEvent(patientUid, actorUsername)` published via `ApplicationEventPublisher` from inpatient's own service; the existing `PatientClosureListener` handles it with no changes.

9. **Concurrency**: `@Version` on `AuditableEntity` — optimistic lock only. No `@Lock(PESSIMISTIC_WRITE)`, no `STALE_ENTITY` ErrorCode (absent from `ErrorCode.java`, gated behind CR-07-Q3 + ADR-0017). Concurrency IT asserts only no-double-claim correctness invariant.

10. **RBAC**: inpatient surface was ungated in legacy (`@PreAuthorize` commented). HMSCLEAN2 applies ADR-0006 deny-by-default `authenticated()` (net-new hardening, not a parity assertion). Only `ADMISSION-CREATE` is legacy-derivable. No `*-WRITE` or `*-DISCHARGE` privilege codes (purge-loop incompatible per `MainApplication.java:306-338` + `security/Operation.java`).

11. **Admission numbering**: uid surrogate only. No `DocumentNumberService` sequence, no prefix. Confirmed: `PatientResource.java:5183-5211` — no number generation.

12. **Consultation block while admitted** (`PatientResource.java:531-533`): outpatient consultation creation hard-rejects when patient has a PENDING/IN-PROCESS admission.

13. **Audit**: every create/approve/sign-out on Admission, DischargePlan, ReferralPlan, DeceasedNote recorded via `shared.audit.AuditRecorder` (ADR-0007 SHA-256 chained). Not Envers.

**Flyway migrations:** V44 (Admission + AdmissionBed), V45 (DischargePlan + DeceasedNote inpatient columns). The existing `referral_plans` table/V28 is reused as-is for the inpatient referral path.

**What is explicitly NOT built in 07a:**
- No `WardTransfer` entity / `/transfer-ward` / `TRANSFERRED` state (CR-07-ward-transfer)
- No MAR / `MedicationAdministration` (CR-07-MAR)
- No `STALE_ENTITY` ErrorCode or `OptimisticLockException` handler (CR-07-Q3 + ADR-0017)
- No second-approver SoD gate / `SELF_APPROVAL_FORBIDDEN` (CR-07-SoD)
- No admit-time deceased guard / `PATIENT_DECEASED` ErrorCode / `Patient.deceased` boolean (CR-07-deceased-guard)
- No `ExternalMedicalProvider` masterdata (CR-INC07-Q7)
- No `billsCleared` denormalized field — live-recompute only
- No `AdmissionBed.closedAt` set at discharge (CR-07-Q10 — legacy leak reproduced verbatim)
- No ward-day accrual job / `@Scheduled` / ShedLock (CR-07-Q2 + ADR-0018)
- No consumable chart (07c scope)
- No `businessDayId` on Admission

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: GIVEN a patient with no open admission, WHEN POST /api/v1/inpatient/admissions is called with a valid active EMPTY wardBed uid, THEN: (1) a new Admission row is persisted with status=PENDING; (2) WardBed.status becomes WAITING; (3) a PatientBill is created with billItem='Bed', description='Ward Bed / Room', status=UNPAID, qty=1, amount=WardType.price (NUMERIC 2dp); (4) an AdmissionBed row is created with status=OPENED, openedAt set to server timestamp, closedAt=NULL, patientBillUid=the ward bill uid; (5) Patient.type becomes INPATIENT; (6) an AuditRecorder entry is written for the Admission CREATE; (7) response is HTTP 201.

GIVEN a patient who already has an Admission in status PENDING, WHEN POST /api/v1/inpatient/admissions is attempted for that same patient, THEN the request is rejected with HTTP 422 and the response body contains the verbatim message 'Could not process admission. The patient is already admitted'.

GIVEN a patient who already has an Admission in status IN-PROCESS, WHEN POST /api/v1/inpatient/admissions is attempted for that same patient, THEN the request is rejected with HTTP 422 and the response body contains the verbatim message 'Could not process admission. The patient is already admitted'.

GIVEN a WardBed where active=false, WHEN POST /api/v1/inpatient/admissions is attempted targeting that bed, THEN the request is rejected with HTTP 422 and the response body contains the message 'Could not process admission, bed not active'.

GIVEN a WardBed where status != 'EMPTY' (e.g. WAITING or OCCUPIED), WHEN POST /api/v1/inpatient/admissions is attempted targeting that bed, THEN the request is rejected with HTTP 422 and the response body contains the message 'Could not process admission, bed already occupied. Please select a different bed.'

GIVEN a CASH patient admitted (Admission=PENDING, WardBed=WAITING, wardBed PatientBill=UNPAID), WHEN the cashier pays the ward-bed bill (triggering BillSettledEvent), THEN the AdmissionSettlementListener: (1) sets the patient's PENDING Admission status to IN-PROCESS; (2) sets WardBed.status to OCCUPIED; (3) signs out any IN-PROCESS consultations (Consultation.status=SIGNED-OUT); all three mutations commit atomically with the payment BEFORE_COMMIT.

GIVEN an INSURANCE patient whose insurance plan has a WardTypeInsurancePlan row with covered=true AND eligiblePlan.price == WardType.price (no top-up needed), WHEN doAdmission completes, THEN: (1) Admission.status=IN-PROCESS; (2) WardBed.status=OCCUPIED; (3) the ward PatientBill status=COVERED at eligiblePlan.price; (4) a PENDING insurance PatientInvoice + PatientInvoiceDetail (qty=1) is created; (5) any PENDING/IN-PROCESS consultations are signed out. All within the doAdmission transaction.

GIVEN an INSURANCE patient whose insurance plan resolves a covered WardTypeInsurancePlan where eligiblePlan.insurancePlan.id != patient.insurancePlan.id AND (WardType.price - eligiblePlan.price) > 0, WHEN doAdmission completes, THEN: (1) the principal ward PatientBill is COVERED at eligiblePlan.price; (2) a supplementary PatientBill is created with status=UNPAID, billItem='Bed', description='Ward Bed / Room (Top up)', amount=(WardType.price - eligiblePlan.price); (3) principalBill.supplementaryBill and supplementaryBill.principalBill are set bidirectionally; (4) the supplementary bill has its own PENDING cash PatientInvoice + PatientInvoiceDetail (qty=1); (5) Admission.status remains PENDING; (6) WardBed.status remains WAITING.

GIVEN a WardTypeInsurancePlan row with active=false but covered=true, WHEN doAdmission is called for a patient whose insurance plan is covered by that row, THEN the active=false row IS included in the eligibility selection (i.e. the inactive-but-covered plan is still eligible), reproducing the legacy active-flag-ignored quirk. The active flag must NOT be added to the findByInsurancePlanAndCovered query predicate.

GIVEN an active PENDING admission, WHEN any nursing/charting endpoint is called (e.g. POST dressing chart, POST vitals with admission context), THEN the operation is rejected with HTTP 422 and the message 'Could not be done. Admission not verified'. GIVEN an IN-PROCESS admission, the same charting endpoint proceeds normally.

GIVEN two concurrent POST /admissions requests targeting the same EMPTY WardBed for different patients (run in parallel threads in a Testcontainers IT), WHEN both transactions commit, THEN exactly one succeeds (WardBed.status=WAITING, one Admission=PENDING) and the other's transaction does NOT commit (OptimisticLockException is raised by @Version), leaving no double-claim. The test asserts only the no-double-claim correctness invariant — it does NOT assert a 409 STALE_ENTITY response code (STALE_ENTITY is absent from ErrorCode and no OptimisticLockException handler exists in GlobalExceptionHandler per inc-08 CR-08-Q4).

WHEN GET /api/v1/inpatient/worklist/doctor is called, THEN the response contains only admissions with status IN-PROCESS or STOPPED. PENDING admissions do NOT appear. WHEN GET /api/v1/inpatient/worklist/nurse is called, THEN only IN-PROCESS admissions appear.

GIVEN a patient with an active PENDING or IN-PROCESS admission, WHEN a new outpatient consultation creation is attempted for that patient, THEN the request is rejected with HTTP 422 (reproducing the legacy cross-context guard 'Could not process consultation, the patient has an active admission').

GIVEN an IN-PROCESS admission with no outstanding UNPAID or VERIFIED PatientBills, WHEN POST /api/v1/inpatient/admissions/{uid}/discharge-plan is called, THEN: (1) Admission.status becomes STOPPED; (2) a DischargePlan with status=PENDING is created; (3) no bills-cleared check is performed at create time; (4) HTTP 201 is returned.

GIVEN a STOPPED admission whose bills-cleared scan finds at least one PatientBill with status UNPAID, WHEN GET /api/v1/inpatient/admissions/{uid}/discharge-summary is called, THEN the request is rejected with HTTP 422 and the message 'Could not get discharge summary. Patient have uncleared bills.'

GIVEN a STOPPED admission whose bills-cleared scan finds at least one PatientBill with status VERIFIED (e.g. an accrued insurance ward bill), WHEN GET /api/v1/inpatient/admissions/{uid}/discharge-summary is called, THEN the request is also rejected with HTTP 422 and the message 'Could not get discharge summary. Patient have uncleared bills.' (VERIFIED counts as outstanding, not cleared).

GIVEN a STOPPED admission with all PatientBills in a cleared state (none UNPAID, none VERIFIED), WHEN GET /api/v1/inpatient/admissions/{uid}/discharge-summary is called, THEN: (1) all admission PatientInvoices are set to APPROVED; (2) Admission.status becomes SIGNED-OUT; (3) WardBed.status becomes EMPTY; (4) Patient.type becomes OUTPATIENT; (5) Patient.paymentType becomes CASH; (6) Patient.insurancePlanUid is cleared to null; (7) dischargedBy/On/At are stamped from DischargePlan.createdBy/On/At; (8) DischargePlan.status becomes APPROVED with approvedBy=DischargePlan.createdBy (single-actor copy); (9) Admission.dischargedAt is NOT null; (10) AuditRecorder records the APPROVE action; (11) HTTP 200 is returned.

GIVEN a STOPPED admission that is successfully discharged (SIGNED-OUT), THEN the final AdmissionBed row remains with status=OPENED and closedAt=NULL (legacy leak reproduced verbatim; CR-07-Q10 parked). An assertion that 'all terminal admissions have CLOSED AdmissionBed rows' MUST NOT be added to the test suite.

GIVEN an IN-PROCESS admission, WHEN POST /api/v1/inpatient/admissions/{uid}/referral-plan is called with a non-null externalMedicalProviderUid, THEN: (1) Admission.status becomes STOPPED; (2) an inpatient ReferralPlan is created (writing the existing referral_plans table with admissionUid set, consultationUid null) with status=PENDING; (3) HTTP 201 is returned.

WHEN POST /api/v1/inpatient/admissions/{uid}/referral-plan is called with externalMedicalProviderUid=null or blank, THEN the request is rejected with HTTP 400 (Bean Validation @NotBlank on externalMedicalProviderUid). No FK existence check is performed — the uid is accepted verbatim.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: GIVEN a STOPPED admission with cleared bills, WHEN GET /api/v1/inpatient/admissions/{uid}/referral-summary is called, THEN: (1) all admission PatientInvoices approved; (2) Admission.status=SIGNED-OUT; (3) WardBed=EMPTY; (4) patient reset to OUTPATIENT/CASH/no-plan; (5) ReferralPlan.status=APPROVED with approvedBy=createdBy; (6) dischargedBy/On/At stamped; (7) AdmissionBed left OPENED (legacy leak); (8) AuditRecorder APPROVE entry written.

GIVEN an IN-PROCESS admission with a non-null patientSummary and causeOfDeath, WHEN POST /api/v1/inpatient/admissions/{uid}/deceased-note is called, THEN: (1) Admission.status becomes HELD; (2) WardBed.status becomes EMPTY (bed freed at HELD, before summary approval); (3) DeceasedNote is created with status=PENDING; (4) the user-supplied date and time are stored as separate columns (NOT a server timestamp); (5) HTTP 201 is returned.

WHEN POST /api/v1/inpatient/admissions/{uid}/deceased-note is called with null patientSummary or null causeOfDeath, THEN the request is rejected with HTTP 400 (Bean Validation @NotBlank). Both fields are mandatory at create time.

GIVEN a HELD admission with cleared bills, WHEN GET /api/v1/inpatient/admissions/{uid}/deceased-summary is called, THEN: (1) all admission PatientInvoices approved; (2) Admission.status=SIGNED-OUT; (3) WardBed=EMPTY; (4) Patient.type=DECEASED (achieved by publishing PatientDeceasedEvent(patientUid, actorUsername) in-tx via ApplicationEventPublisher — the existing PatientClosureListener flips the type BEFORE_COMMIT); (5) DeceasedNote.status=APPROVED with approvedBy=createdBy copy; (6) Admission.dischargedAt REMAINS NULL (deceased path does NOT stamp dischargedAt/By/On on Admission); (7) AuditRecorder APPROVE entry written; (8) ApplicationModules.verify() remains green (no new inpatient<->registration compile edge).

GIVEN a deceased-note finalization that publishes PatientDeceasedEvent, WHEN the existing PatientClosureListener.onPatientDeceased (BEFORE_COMMIT) handles it, THEN Patient.type is set to DECEASED atomically within the same transaction. The inpatient service does NOT call registration code directly (no inpatient→registration compile edge). No duplicate PatientDeceasedEvent type or second listener is created.

GIVEN a discharge summary, referral summary, or deceased summary finalization, THEN approvedBy is always set equal to the DischargePlan/ReferralPlan/DeceasedNote createdBy (single-actor copy — no second-approver check, no approvedBy != createdBy guard, no SELF_APPROVAL_FORBIDDEN ErrorCode).

GIVEN any inpatient endpoint, WHEN called without a valid JWT, THEN HTTP 401 is returned (ADR-0006 deny-by-default authenticated() hardening — net-new; legacy was ungated). This is labelled as net-new RBAC hardening, NOT a parity assertion.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: The Admission entity carries no database sequence number and no document-number prefix. The uid (ULID) is the only externally meaningful identifier. DocumentNumberService is NOT called for admissions.

The AdmissionStatus enum is frozen at exactly five values: PENDING, IN-PROCESS, STOPPED, HELD, SIGNED-OUT. The value TRANSFERRED must not exist. AdmissionBed status is CHECK-constrained to exactly OPENED and CLOSED.

Flyway migrations V44 and V45 are idempotent, reviewed by the data-architect, and produce tables: admissions, admission_beds (V44) and discharge_plans, deceased_notes with inpatient columns on referral_plans (V45). Migrations start at V44 (V43 is the last inc-08 migration). No schema change exists outside a versioned Flyway script.

The AdmissionSettlementListener in the inpatient module consumes BillSettledEvent with @TransactionalEventListener(phase=BEFORE_COMMIT). It imports only shared.event. It does NOT modify ConsultationSettlementListener. ApplicationModules.verify() passes with no new inpatient<->billing compile edge beyond the shared.event channel.

The inpatient ward-bed bill is created directly by the inpatient AdmissionService — NOT via BillingCommands.recordClinicalCharge — because the ward admission billing requires the custom COVERED/top-up mutation logic (PatientServiceImpl.java:1795-1966) that the current ChargeRequest/PriceLookupImpl surface does not expose (PriceLookupImpl.java:33-38 explicitly defers the top-up split). The ward PatientBill is created with kind=WARD, patientUid, businessDayUid, and all other AuditableEntity columns set.

The Admission entity's wardBed column in the Flyway DDL (V44) is NOT marked non-updatable at the database level (i.e. no updatable=false on the FK column). Legacy Admission.java:62-65 shows updatable=true. Although no business logic mutates wardBed post-admission, the DDL must reflect the actual legacy column attribute.

GIVEN any create/approve/sign-out mutation on Admission, DischargePlan, ReferralPlan (inpatient path), or DeceasedNote, THEN shared.audit.AuditRecorder records the action (entity name, uid, AuditAction, actor username) within the same transaction. No Hibernate Envers @Audited annotation is present on any inpatient entity.

The GET /api/v1/inpatient/admissions/{uid}/discharge-summary bills-cleared scan checks ALL PatientBills linked to the admission's invoices and rejects if ANY bill has status UNPAID OR VERIFIED. Status COVERED and PAID are considered cleared. This is a live scan — no billsCleared denormalized field exists on Admission.

check.section: 07a — Two-phase admission lifecycle + admit guards + payment-driven activation
check.reason: The section is overwhelmingly charter-compliant: it reproduces the verified two-phase admission lifecycle, the payment-gated activation, the active-flag-ignored insurance eligibility + load-bearing top-up split, the AdmissionBed leak, single-actor approvedBy=createdBy approval, the deceased terminal via the existing PatientDeceasedEvent/PatientClosureListener, the loose external_medical_provider_uid (no FK), the verbatim 422 admit-guard messages, uid-only admission numbering, ADR-0006 deny-by-default labelled as net-new (not parity), and the @Version-only concurrency posture. It does NOT smuggle any of the named phantoms (no DischargePlan.kind, no WardTransfer/TRANSFERRED, no SoD/SELF_APPROVAL_FORBIDDEN, no admit-time deceased guard/PATIENT_DECEASED/Patient.deceased, no real provider FK, no MAR, no consumable stock decrement, no Q11-bug fixes), and the two genuinely blocked items (AC-ADM-36 @Scheduled accrual cron; AC-ADM-37 STALE_ENTITY/409 + optimistic-lock handler) are correctly marked frozen:false behind the right gates (CR-07-Q2/ADR-0018 and CR-07-Q3/ADR-0017). I verified ErrorCode.java (no STALE_ENTITY/PATIENT_DECEASED/SELF_APPROVAL_FORBIDDEN; BUSINESS_RULE=422, VALIDATION=400) and GlobalExceptionHandler.java (no ObjectOptimisticLockingFailureException handler), confirming the blocked-item framing. The single charter breach is AC-ADM-23, which re-homes a legacy SERVICE-BODY business-rule validation into a DTO Bean-Validation @NotBlank, silently changing the HTTP status from the legacy 422 to 400 and replacing the specific legacy message with a generic VALIDATION body. Because this asserts a wrong status code/error contract as frozen:true parity, the section must be revised. AC-ADM-20 carries the same 422-vs-400 pattern but is defensible (Q7 explicitly ratified @NotBlank as the mechanism) and is downgraded to advisory. Verdict: REVISE for AC-ADM-23.
corrections: BLOCKING — AC-ADM-23 is a silent status-code + error-contract drift. Legacy validates patientSummary/causeOfDeath in the service body and throws InvalidOperationException 'Summary and cause of death are missing' (PatientResource.java:5699-5766; 00-discovery-raw.json discharge-plan finding at line 270 and synthesis.groundTruthModel 'DeceasedNote validates patientSummary + causeOfDeath mandatory'). In HMSCLEAN2 that maps to ErrorCode.BUSINESS_RULE = HTTP 422 (verified ErrorCode.java:16). The draft instead asserts HTTP 400 via Bean Validation @NotBlank (MethodArgumentNotValidException -> ErrorCode.VALIDATION = 400, verified GlobalExceptionHandler.java:55-70 / ErrorCode.java:17), changing both the status code (422->400) and the response body (specific legacy message -> generic 'Validation failed' field-error list). The Q6 adversarial challenge (02-decisions-raw.json line 119) explicitly lists 'patientSummary+causeOfDeath validation' as a 07a parity build item to reproduce. Rewrite AC-ADM-23 to assert: null/blank patientSummary OR causeOfDeath is rejected with HTTP 422 mapped to BUSINESS_RULE, carrying the verbatim legacy message 'Summary and cause of death are missing', enforced in the inpatient application service (not a DTO @NotBlank), frozen:true. Update the AC-ADM-22 narrative (which references @NotBlank) to match.

ADVISORY — AC-ADM-20 asserts HTTP 400 (Bean Validation @NotBlank) for a blank externalMedicalProviderUid. Legacy enforced the mandatory-provider rule via a non-null FK with a 404-if-missing/reject path (00-discovery-raw.json discharge-plan finding at line 250/255; synthesis.groundTruthModel 'ReferralPlan has a @ManyToOne FK to ExternalMedicalProvider (nullable=false)'). The ratified Q7 decision (03-DECISIONS-RATIFIED.md Q7; 02-decisions-raw.json line 128) deliberately keeps a loose NOT NULL + @NotBlank uid with no FK and no existence check, so a 400 is a defensible consequence of the ratified @NotBlank mechanism — but the legacy mandatory-rejection contract was not a 400. Either keep 400 (acceptable, since Q7 named @NotBlank) but drop any implication that 400 is legacy parity, or for cross-disposition consistency with the corrected AC-ADM-23 enforce the mandatory rule in-service as a 422. Flag this as a documented modern-validation consequence, not legacy fidelity.

ADVISORY — AC-ADM-13 reproduces the outpatient-consultation-while-admitted guard (PatientResource.java:531-533, message 'Could not process consultation, the patient has an active admission', confirmed 00-discovery-raw.json line 114). This guard lives on the OUTPATIENT do_consultation endpoint, which is owned by the clinical/registration context (and was one of the 7 actively-@PreAuthorize-gated legacy endpoints), not by inpatient 07a. Clarify in the AC that 07a does not edit the clinical consultation-create flow; if the guard must read inpatient admission state it should do so via a published clinical<->inpatient seam, and ApplicationModules.verify() must stay green. Escalate the exact ownership/seam to the solution-architect rather than implying 07a mutates the clinical endpoint.

ADVISORY — AC-ADM-32 specifies the inpatient AdmissionService creates the ward PatientBill DIRECTLY (bypassing BillingCommands.recordClinicalCharge) to carry the custom COVERED/top-up mutation that PriceLookupImpl explicitly defers (00-discovery-raw.json alreadyBuilt: 'PriceLookupImpl doc :30-38 ... top-up split ... explicitly NOT implemented'). The process reproduced is faithful, but bypassing the published billing charge seam is an architecture/seam decision, not a process-fidelity element — it should be explicitly confirmed by the solution-architect (and the resulting module-boundary checked with ApplicationModules.verify()), since the reviewer must not author the architectural choice. Add a note that this seam choice is owner/architect-confirmed, not a legacy parity assertion.

ADVISORY (non-blocking, citation precision) — AC-ADM-11's legacyCite ('PatientServiceImpl.java:1703-1711 bed EMPTY check + WAITING set') is the correct behavioural anchor, but the no-double-claim invariant is enforced by @Version on AuditableEntity (a modern data-model mechanism), not by a legacy concurrency primitive (legacy has NO pessimistic lock anywhere — synthesis.alreadyBuilt and reconciliation §Shared kernel). Keep the AC, but label the @Version optimistic-lock invariant as the modern-mechanism reproduction of correctness rather than a legacy parity cite, to avoid implying legacy had commit-time lock semantics.

section.section: 07a — Ward pricing: cash + insurance eligibility + top-up split (Q9)
section.overview:
## 07a Ward Pricing — Cash + Insurance Eligibility + Load-Bearing Top-Up Split

**Sub-increment:** 07a (Admission + Discharge + Ward).

### What this slice builds

The ward-bed billing engine that must execute inside `doAdmission` in the new `inpatient` module. It is the only path that activates the `PENDING → IN-PROCESS` gate (no payment → stays PENDING; full insurance cover OR ward-bed bill paid → IN-PROCESS/OCCUPIED). The slice has three tightly coupled concerns:

1. **Cash ward bill** — create one `PatientBill` at admit time: `amount = WardType.price`, `qty = 1`, `billItem = "Bed"`, `description = "Ward Bed / Room"`, `status = UNPAID`.
2. **Insurance eligibility loop** — for INSURANCE patients, query covered plans via `findByInsurancePlanAndCovered(plan, true)`, choose the eligible plan by max-price-OR-exact-plan-match, and set the principal bill `COVERED`. The `.active` flag on `WardTypeInsurancePlan` is **intentionally ignored** — an inactive-but-covered plan stays eligible verbatim (honouring `.active` is CR-07-Q9, frozen:false).
3. **Load-bearing top-up split** — if `eligiblePlan` differs from the patient's own plan **AND** `(WardType.price − eligiblePlan.price) > 0`, create a supplementary `PatientBill` (`amount = WardType.price − eligiblePlan.price`, `status = UNPAID`, `description = "Ward Bed / Room (Top up)"`). This supplementary bill's UNPAID status is the gate that keeps the admission `PENDING/WAITING` until it is paid.

### Legacy-accurate behaviour

- **Admission-time bill status distinction is load-bearing:** the admission-time bill is `UNPAID`; each accrued (subsequent-24h) bill is `VERIFIED`; an insurance-covered bill is `COVERED` (`PatientServiceImpl.java:1760`, `UpdatePatient.java:304-311`).
- **No-top-up path:** eligible plan exactly covers WardType.price → admission flips to `IN-PROCESS` / bed to `OCCUPIED` immediately inside `doAdmission` (`PatientServiceImpl.java:1950-1963`). This is the only path that activates at admit.
- **Top-up path:** supplementary `UNPAID` bill created; admission stays `PENDING`, bed stays `WAITING`. Activation occurs later when the ward-bed bill is paid — the payment handler finds all PENDING admissions for the patient and promotes each to `IN-PROCESS` and its bed to `OCCUPIED` (`PatientBillResource.java:352-365`).
- **Cash path:** same as top-up: stays `PENDING/WAITING` until the UNPAID ward-bed bill is paid.
- **`WardType.price` is the single cash anchor** — a flat `BigDecimal NUMERIC(19,2)` on `WardType` (`WardType.java:39-40`). There is NO per-day rate, no per-ward price. `AdmissionBed` (occupancy/billing ledger, `status OPENED/CLOSED`, `openedAt/closedAt`, `@OneToOne PatientBill`) is the per-period backing record for accrual.

### Deferred gap the inpatient service must fill

`PriceLookupImpl.java:30-38` explicitly documents that the ward referral-override + top-up split is **NOT implemented** in the `masterdata` pricing bean — it is a load-bearing gap that `07a` must fill, either inline in the inpatient service or by adding a narrow ward-pricing helper within the `inpatient` module. The `billing::api.recordClinicalCharge` / `ChargeRequest` seam carries `inpatient(boolean)` and `planUid` but has **no top-up or multi-bill API**; the inpatient service must write the two `PatientBill` rows directly via the billing seam's two calls (principal + supplementary), or — because `ChargeRequest` has no `billItem`/`description` fields — write them directly as inpatient-owned `PatientBill` domain writes inside the inpatient module's transaction, mirroring the legacy inline approach in `PatientServiceImpl`. Either approach is acceptable; the key invariant is that two separate bills are persisted with the correct status and amounts.

### Money precision

All monetary fields: `BigDecimal NUMERIC(19,2)` with `HALF_UP` rounding at 2dp (pre-approved double→BigDecimal replacement). Top-up amount: `wardType.price.subtract(eligiblePlan.price).setScale(2, HALF_UP)`.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: GIVEN a cash patient admitted to a ward, WHEN doAdmission executes, THEN exactly one PatientBill is created with amount=WardType.price (NUMERIC 19,2, HALF_UP), qty=1, billItem='Bed', description='Ward Bed / Room', status=UNPAID, and exactly one AdmissionBed row is created with status=OPENED and openedAt set to the current timestamp.
GIVEN a cash patient, WHEN doAdmission completes, THEN Admission.status=PENDING and WardBed.status=WAITING (the no-activation path for cash patients).

GIVEN an INSURANCE patient whose active insurance plan has a fully-covering WardTypeInsurancePlan row (covered=true, eligiblePlan.price >= WardType.price), WHEN doAdmission executes, THEN the principal PatientBill is created with status=COVERED, amount=eligiblePlan.price, and Admission.status is set to IN-PROCESS and WardBed.status to OCCUPIED within the same transaction (the no-top-up activation path).

GIVEN an INSURANCE patient whose eligible WardTypeInsurancePlan row has price P where P < WardType.price, WHEN doAdmission executes, THEN (a) a principal PatientBill is created with status=COVERED and amount=P, AND (b) a supplementary PatientBill is created with status=UNPAID, amount=(WardType.price - P) rounded HALF_UP to 2dp, description='Ward Bed / Room (Top up)', AND (c) Admission.status remains PENDING and WardBed.status remains WAITING (top-up branch does NOT activate at admit).

GIVEN an INSURANCE patient in PENDING/WAITING state (top-up or cash path), WHEN the ward-bed bill is paid (PatientBillResource payment handler fires), THEN ALL of the patient's PENDING admissions transition to IN-PROCESS and their associated WardBeds transition to OCCUPIED.

GIVEN an INSURANCE patient, WHEN the insurance eligibility loop runs, THEN it queries WardTypeInsurancePlan using findByInsurancePlanAndCovered(plan, true) and selects the eligible plan by (i) breaking on an exact plan-id match if found, else (ii) taking the row with the highest price among covered rows. The WardTypeInsurancePlan.active flag is NOT included in the query predicate and NOT checked in the selection loop — an inactive-but-covered plan is eligible verbatim.

GIVEN an INSURANCE patient where no covered WardTypeInsurancePlan row exists for the plan, WHEN doAdmission executes, THEN the admission falls back to the cash path: one UNPAID PatientBill at WardType.price, Admission.status=PENDING, WardBed.status=WAITING.

GIVEN a top-up split has created both a COVERED principal bill (amount=eligiblePlan.price) and an UNPAID supplementary bill (amount=WardType.price - eligiblePlan.price), THEN the sum of the two amounts equals WardType.price at 2dp (HALF_UP).

GIVEN the active-flag-ignored eligibility behaviour is the frozen baseline, WHEN an admin marks a WardTypeInsurancePlan row as inactive (active=false) but it remains covered=true for the patient's plan, THEN doAdmission still selects that row and creates the COVERED bill (not a cash fallback). Asserting this behaviour as POSITIVE is required; correcting it is CR-07-Q9 (parked).

GIVEN the top-up branch (supplementary UNPAID bill exists), WHEN a discharge gate check runs (getDischargeSummary/getReferralSummary/getDeceasedSummary), THEN the supplementary bill's UNPAID status causes the gate to reject with 'Patient have uncleared bills.' if it has not been paid — confirming the top-up bill participates in the bills-cleared gate.

GIVEN doAdmission runs for any payment path, THEN the WardType.price source is WardBed.ward.wardType.price (the single BigDecimal NUMERIC(19,2) field on WardType). No per-ward price, no per-day rate, no ServicePrice table lookup is used for the admission-time cash price.

GIVEN the accrual oracle context (07c parity baseline): the admission-time bill has status UNPAID; each accrued (subsequent rolling-24h) bill has status VERIFIED; an insurance-covered bill has status COVERED. The discharge gate treats BOTH UNPAID and VERIFIED as outstanding (blocks sign-out). This status distinction is load-bearing for the gate and must be preserved verbatim.

GIVEN the WardTypeInsurancePlan eligibility loop implementation, THEN PriceLookupImpl.resolve() is NOT called for the ward admission-time principal bill. The inpatient service implements the legacy max-price-OR-exact-match loop directly against the WardTypeInsurancePlan repository (or an equivalent inpatient-owned helper), because PriceLookupImpl.java:30-38 explicitly documents this gap as deferred billing-engine work not covered by the masterdata lookup bean.

GIVEN the honouring-active-flag deviation is CR-07-Q9 (parked), WHEN the build spec is reviewed, THEN no code path checks WardTypeInsurancePlan.active before considering a covered row eligible. Any attempt to add an `.active == true` filter to the eligibility query is a blocked deviation that must not pass code review without CR-07-Q9 being approved.

GIVEN the Flyway migration sequence (inc-08 ended at V43), WHEN the inpatient module migrations are applied, THEN the admission_beds table (AdmissionBed entity: uid ULID PK, status VARCHAR(20), opened_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL, closed_at TIMESTAMP NULL, ward_bed_id FK to ward_beds NOT NULL, admission_id FK to admissions NOT NULL, patient_bill_id FK to patient_bills NOT NULL UNIQUE) is created in a migration numbered V44 or higher. No WardTypeInsurancePlan DDL change is introduced (the existing service_prices table with kind=WARD already serves the insurance pricing seam per CR-04).

GIVEN an INSURANCE patient where the eligible plan's price exactly equals WardType.price (zero top-up difference), WHEN doAdmission executes, THEN only ONE PatientBill is created (COVERED, amount=WardType.price=eligiblePlan.price) and NO supplementary top-up bill is created. Admission activates immediately to IN-PROCESS/OCCUPIED.

GIVEN two concurrent doAdmission calls targeting the same EMPTY WardBed, WHEN both transactions commit, THEN exactly one succeeds (WardBed transitions to WAITING), and the losing transaction does NOT commit (no double-claim). The @Version optimistic lock on WardBed (inherited from AuditableEntity.java:77-79) prevents the double-claim at the persistence layer. The concurrency IT asserts only the no-double-claim correctness invariant — NOT a 409 STALE_ENTITY response (STALE_ENTITY ErrorCode is absent from as-built ErrorCode.java; clean 409 surfacing is parked behind CR-07-Q3 + ADR-0017).

GIVEN the as-built billing seam (ChargeRequest has no billItem/description fields), WHEN the inpatient service creates the ward-bed PatientBill for COVERED/UNPAID cases, THEN the bill's billItem and description are set to 'Bed' and 'Ward Bed / Room' respectively (for the principal bill) and 'Ward Bed / Room (Top up)' (for the supplementary bill). These literals must be written directly on the inpatient-owned PatientBill write (not via recordClinicalCharge), since ChargeRequest carries no display-label fields.

GIVEN the active-flag-ignored rule (CR-07-Q9 is parked and NOT approved), WHEN a test fixture creates both an active and an inactive covered WardTypeInsurancePlan for the same patient plan, THEN the inactive row is still returned by findByInsurancePlanAndCovered(plan, true) and is eligible for the selection loop — the repository query has no active=true predicate.

blockedItems: AC-WP-14 (frozen:false) — the 'honouring active flag' check CANNOT be built into the baseline without CR-07-Q9 being approved by the engagement-lead. The criterion is written as a code-review guardrail asserting the legacy quirk must be preserved; no gate exists yet to flip it.

Scheduling/accrual cron criteria (not listed here — scoped to 07c) — NO @Scheduled / ShedLock / Quartz may be built until ADR-0018 is ratified by the engagement-lead + scheduling infra is authorized. The accrueWardDay service method parity oracle (AC-WP-12) is freezable; the scheduler binding is not.

STALE_ENTITY 409 surfacing — CR-07-Q3 + ADR-0017 ratification required before the OptimisticLockException handler and STALE_ENTITY ErrorCode are added to the shared kernel. AC-WP-17 is intentionally scoped to the no-double-claim invariant only.
Active-flag fix (CR-07-Q9) — engagement-lead must approve before any .active predicate is added to the WardTypeInsurancePlan eligibility query. HDE recommends approval but this is an owner call.
cites: PatientServiceImpl.java:1753-1783 — admission-time PatientBill creation (cash path, UNPAID, WardType.price)
PatientServiceImpl.java:1795-1819 — insurance eligibility loop (findByInsurancePlanAndCovered, max-price/exact-match selection, COVERED bill)
PatientServiceImpl.java:1880-1897 — supplementary top-up PatientBill (UNPAID, WardType.price - eligiblePlan.price, 'Ward Bed / Room (Top up)')
PatientServiceImpl.java:1950-1963 — no-top-up full-cover activation: Admission.status=IN-PROCESS, WardBed.status=OCCUPIED
PatientServiceImpl.java:1713-1786 — doAdmission full method: PENDING admission + WAITING bed + AdmissionBed OPENED
PatientServiceImpl.java:1754-1756 — WardType.price cash anchor source (wb.getWard().getWardType().getPrice())
PatientBillResource.java:352-365 — payment-driven activation (PENDING→IN-PROCESS, WAITING→OCCUPIED) for cash/top-up paths

WardTypeInsurancePlan.java:43-44 — active field exists on entity but is never queried in eligibility
