check.section: 07a — Ward pricing: cash + insurance eligibility + top-up split (Q9)
check.reason: The section is charter-clean on every headline smuggling trap (no DischargePlan.kind, no WardTransfer/TRANSFERRED, no SoD/SELF_APPROVAL_FORBIDDEN, no admit-time deceased guard/PATIENT_DECEASED/Patient.deceased, no STALE_ENTITY/409 as frozen, no pessimistic lock, no honouring of WardTypeInsurancePlan.active, no close-AdmissionBed-at-discharge, no consumable stock decrement, no Q11 bug-fixes, no real ExternalMedicalProvider FK, no MAR, no scheduler-as-frozen, no ADMISSION sequence), the sub-increment is correctly 07a, the BLOCKED items (CR-07-Q9 active-flag, CR-07-Q2 cron, CR-07-Q3 lock+STALE_ENTITY) are correctly marked, and every frozen:true criterion is backed by a verified legacy file:line cite (I confirmed 1753-1783, 1795-1819, 1880-1897, 1950-1963, 5342-5357, UpdatePatient.java:304-311, WardType.java:39-40, WardTypeInsurancePlanRepository.java:40, legacy AdmissionBed.java:40-68, and as-built AuditableEntity.java:77-79 @Version + ErrorCode.java having no STALE_ENTITY). HOWEVER there is a BLOCKING behaviour-drift defect: AC-WP-03, AC-WP-04, AC-WP-08 and AC-WP-16 misstate the admit-time activation gate. Legacy activation (the else at PatientServiceImpl.java:1950, which is the negation of the line-1880 top-up guard) fires when eligiblePlan.insurancePlan.id == patient.insurancePlan.id OR (WardType.price - eligiblePlan.price) <= 0 — a TWO-part condition whose primary discriminator is PLAN-IDENTITY, not price coverage. Because findByInsurancePlanAndCovered(p.getInsurancePlan(), true) (repo line 40) already restricts every returned row to the patient's OWN plan, the plan-id half is always true, the selection loop short-circuits on the first row (break at 1805-1807), the line-1880 top-up guard is (near-)unreachable, and the admission ACTIVATES to IN-PROCESS/OCCUPIED at admit with NO top-up bill EVEN WHEN eligiblePlan.price < WardType.price. The draft instead characterizes activation as 'eligiblePlan.price >= WardType.price' (AC-WP-03) and routes any price-below-cost own-plan admission into the PENDING/WAITING top-up path (AC-WP-04) with a positive supplementary-bill golden-master (AC-WP-08), which inverts the most common insurance path and contradicts the ratified Q9 challenge refinement #1 (02-decisions-raw.json:180: 'an own-plan covered admission yields NO top-up and DOES flip IN-PROCESS/OCCUPIED at admit'). This is silent billing/lifecycle drift and must be corrected before freeze.
corrections: AC-WP-03 (BLOCKING — wrong activation discriminator): replace the activation predicate 'eligiblePlan.price >= WardType.price' with the verbatim legacy two-part condition. Activation at admit (IN-PROCESS / OCCUPIED, inside doAdmission) occurs in the line-1950 else branch, i.e. when NOT(eligiblePlan.insurancePlan.id != patient.insurancePlan.id AND (WardType.price - eligiblePlan.price) > 0) — equivalently when eligiblePlan IS the patient's own plan OR (WardType.price - eligiblePlan.price) <= 0. The primary discriminator is PLAN IDENTITY, not price. Cite PatientServiceImpl.java:1880 (guard) and :1950-1963 (activation else).

AC-WP-04 (BLOCKING — top-up path mischaracterized as price-only and routinely reachable): the supplementary UNPAID top-up bill + PENDING/WAITING no-activation is gated on BOTH conditions: eligiblePlan.insurancePlan.id != patient.insurancePlan.id AND (WardType.price - eligiblePlan.price) > 0 (PatientServiceImpl.java:1880). Remove the implication that 'eligiblePlan.price < WardType.price' alone triggers the top-up/PENDING path. Add the load-bearing note (per ratified Q9 challenge refinement #1) that because findByInsurancePlanAndCovered(p.getInsurancePlan(), true) restricts rows to the patient's own plan, the plan-id-differs half is (near-)unreachable in practice — the top-up branch must be BUILT verbatim but is not expected to fire under the legacy query scope. Do NOT 'fix' the query scope to make it reachable.

AC-WP-08 (BLOCKING — false positive golden-master): reframe so the canonical insurance golden-master is an OWN-PLAN covered admission that yields NO supplementary bill and DOES activate IN-PROCESS/OCCUPIED at admit (the legacy-typical path). The two-bill sum-equals-WardType.price assertion must be scoped explicitly to the (near-)unreachable plan-id-differs-AND-diff>0 branch and labelled as such, so QA does not treat an empty top-up as a build defect (per 02-decisions-raw.json:180). As written it asserts a supplementary bill as the expected outcome of any under-covering plan, which inverts parity.

AC-WP-16 (clarify — activation reason): keep the zero-top-up single-COVERED-bill outcome, but correct the activation rationale: activation here is driven by the line-1880 guard being false (own-plan match short-circuit OR diff<=0), NOT by price equality per se. The 'eligiblePlan.price exactly equals WardType.price' framing is one sufficient case (diff==0) but is not the operative legacy condition; cite the >0 guard at :1880 and the activation else at :1950.

AC-WP-04 / AC-WP-18 (ADVISORY — missing load-bearing linkage): add an explicit criterion that the principal<->supplementary linkage is persisted bidirectionally (legacy setPrincipalPatientBill at PatientServiceImpl.java:1889 on the supplementary bill, and setSupplementaryPatientBill at :1896 on the principal). Ratified Q9 buildImpact item (d) flags this linkage as a mandatory 07a build item; it is currently unspecified.

AC-WP-15 (ADVISORY — incomplete DDL): the legacy AdmissionBed (AdmissionBed.java:40-68) also carries a mandatory patient FK (@JoinColumn patient_id, optional=false, nullable=false, updatable=false) in addition to ward_bed/admission/patient_bill. The draft DDL omits the patient_id column. Add it for relationship-preservation completeness, and per ratified Q10 buildImpact mark the ward_bed/patient_bill/admission/patient join columns updatable=false to mirror legacy.

AC-WP-09 / AC-WP-14 / AC-WP-19 (ADVISORY — redundancy, non-blocking): AC-WP-09, AC-WP-14 and AC-WP-19 all restate the same active-flag-ignored quirk + CR-07-Q9 guardrail. Consider consolidating to one positive parity AC (the inactive-but-covered row is selected) plus the frozen:false guardrail (AC-WP-14), to reduce the chance of divergent edits across three near-duplicate criteria. Substance is correct; this is hygiene only.

section.section: 07a — Disposition Entities + Bills-Cleared Discharge Gate + Single-Actor Approval
section.overview:
## What 07a builds

Sub-increment 07a introduces the three separate disposition entities (`DischargePlan`, `ReferralPlan`, `DeceasedNote`) with their state-machine, the hard inline bills-cleared discharge gate, and single-actor approval. These are the three tables the legacy system uses for end-of-encounter flows — there is **no** typed `kind` discriminator on a shared entity, no second-approver gate, no `billsCleared` stored flag, and no `Patient.deceased` boolean.

### Legacy-accurate behaviour

**DischargePlan** (`discharge_plans` table) is a flat entity with free-text narrative fields `history`, `investigation`, `management`, `operationNote`, `icuAdmissionNote`, `generalRecommendation` (`DischargePlan.java:38-44`). **None of these are mandatory at create**; legacy performs zero required-field validation at save (`PatientResource.java:5259-5286`). Status defaults to `PENDING` and advances only to `APPROVED`. The create path (`save_discharge_plan`) idempotently reuses an existing PENDING plan; on **first** creation it additionally transitions `Admission.status` to `STOPPED`. The modern build maps this to the `inpatient` bounded context and elevates the controller-layer logic to a service method, but the observable side-effects are byte-identical.

**ReferralPlan** (`referral_plans` table) extends the same narrative set with `referringDiagnosis` and a **mandatory** provider reference (`external_medical_provider_uid VARCHAR(26) NOT NULL`). The V28 migration already creates the `referral_plans` table with a MANDATORY loose `external_medical_provider_uid` (no FK — the `external_medical_providers` table is not built; `V28__clinical_closure_deceased_referral.sql:110-111`). Inc-07 maps `inpatient` to that same column, matching the clinical V28 precedent. `ReferralPlan` supports both inpatient **and** outpatient (consultation) via XOR enforced at create time (`PatientResource.java:5402-5407`). The inpatient save path sets `Admission.status = STOPPED`; the outpatient consultation save path runs per-bill-type UNPAID gates over lab/radiology/procedure/prescription bills, then sets `Consultation.status = SIGNED-OUT` and resets `Patient.paymentType = CASH`, `Patient.insurancePlan = null`.

**DeceasedNote** (`deceased_notes` table) has two **mandatory** fields (`patientSummary`, `causeOfDeath`) validated at create (`PatientResource.java:5699-5701`), plus split user-entered `LocalDate date_` / `LocalTime time_` — **not** a server-generated timestamp (`DeceasedNote.java:40-46`; `PatientResource.java:5768-5769`). The deceased flow uses a distinct intermediate state (`IN-PROCESS → HELD`) and **frees the WardBed at the HELD transition** inside `save_deceased_note` — earlier than the discharge/referral paths (`PatientResource.java:5719-5732`). DeceasedNote supports admission AND consultation via XOR.

**Bills-cleared gate** — all three disposition approve paths (`get_discharge_summary`, `get_referral_summary`, `get_deceased_summary`) share the same inline gate: iterate `PatientInvoice → PatientInvoiceDetail → PatientBill.getStatus()` and reject if **any** status equals `"UNPAID"` or `"VERIFIED"` (`PatientResource.java:5342-5352`, `5593-5603`, `5851-5882`). The check is unconditional by payment type; insurance passes because `COVERED` is neither `UNPAID` nor `VERIFIED`. There is **no** stored `billsCleared` flag. On gate pass: all admission invoices are set to `APPROVED` as a side effect before the admission transitions.

**Single-actor approval** — approver is always copied from creator: `approvedBy = createdBy`, `approvedOn = createdOn` (`PatientResource.java:5378-5381` discharge, `5627-5630` referral inpatient, `5679-5681` referral outpatient, `5904-5907` deceased). No `approvedBy != createdBy` check anywhere. `approvedAt` is a server timestamp. All approvals are recorded via `shared.audit.AuditRecorder`.

**Discharge terminal side-effects** (only if `admission.status == "STOPPED"`): `Admission.status → SIGNED-OUT`; `dischargedBy/On/At` copied from the plan's `createdBy/On/At`; `WardBed.status → EMPTY`; `Patient.type → OUTPATIENT`, `Patient.paymentType → CASH`, `Patient.insurancePlan → null` (`PatientResource.java:5354-5385`).

**Deceased terminal** sets `Patient.type → DECEASED` via the **existing** `PatientDeceasedEvent` / `PatientClosureListener` (registration-side, `BEFORE_COMMIT`) — the `inpatient` service publishes the event in-tx; no listener change required. The deceased path does **NOT** stamp `dischargedBy/On/At` (`PatientResource.java:5889-5908`). `AdmissionBed` is **never closed at discharge** — the final `AdmissionBed` row is left `OPENED` (verbatim legacy leak, `UpdatePatient.java:258-334` — accrual rollover is the only closer). This is reproduced exactly; the fix is CR-07-Q10 (parked).

**Flyway scope:** inc-07 migrations start at V44. The `discharge_plans` table is new in V44. The `referral_plans` and `deceased_notes` tables already exist (V28); V44 adds the `admission_uid` loose-ref column to `discharge_plans` only (admission-scoped), and V44 creates the new `admissions` + `admission_beds` tables.

**Out of scope for this section:** MAR (CR-07-MAR), WardTransfer (CR-07-ward-transfer), second-approver SoD (CR-07-SoD), `SELF_APPROVAL_FORBIDDEN` ErrorCode, `PATIENT_DECEASED` ErrorCode, `STALE_ENTITY` ErrorCode, `ADMISSION_BILLS_OUTSTANDING` ErrorCode, `billsCleared` flag, `@Scheduled`/ShedLock accrual cron (CR-07-Q2), pessimistic lock (CR-07-Q3), admit-time deceased guard (CR-07-deceased-guard), close-AdmissionBed-at-discharge (CR-07-Q10).

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: GIVEN a `DischargePlan` entity in the `inpatient` module, WHEN the schema is inspected, THEN table `discharge_plans` has exactly the following columns: `id` (BIGINT PK), `uid` (VARCHAR(26) UNIQUE NOT NULL), `history` (TEXT nullable), `investigation` (TEXT nullable), `management` (TEXT nullable), `operation_note` (TEXT nullable), `icu_admission_note` (TEXT nullable), `general_recommendation` (TEXT nullable), `status` (VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'PENDING'), `admission_uid` (VARCHAR(26) NOT NULL — loose ref, no FK), `approved_by_user_uid` (VARCHAR(26) nullable), `approved_on_day_uid` (VARCHAR(26) nullable), `approved_at` (TIMESTAMPTZ nullable), plus the standard AuditableEntity audit columns (`created_at`, `updated_at`, `created_by`, `updated_by`, `version`); AND a CHECK constraint restricts status to ('PENDING','APPROVED') only; AND a UNIQUE partial index enforces at-most-one plan per admission; Flyway migration V44 is the source.
acceptanceCriteria.decisionRef: Q-disposition (03-DECISIONS-RATIFIED.md: three separate entities, no kind discriminator)

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: GIVEN a `POST /api/v1/inpatient/discharge-plans` request with a valid `admissionUid`, WHEN the admission has no existing `DischargePlan`, THEN: (a) `Admission.status` is set to `STOPPED` and persisted; (b) a new `DischargePlan` with `status = PENDING` is created and returned; (c) `createdBy` / `createdOn` / `createdAt` are stamped from the current actor and business-day; (d) all six narrative fields are copied from the request body verbatim; (e) NO required-field validation is applied to any narrative field; (f) NO bill check is performed at create time.

GIVEN a `POST /api/v1/inpatient/discharge-plans` request for an admission that already has a `DischargePlan` (in any status), WHEN the request is processed, THEN the existing plan is reused (no new row created, `Admission.status` is NOT changed again), and ALL six narrative fields are updated from the request body.

GIVEN a `GET /api/v1/inpatient/discharge-plans/{uid}/approve` request, WHEN the admission has ANY `PatientInvoice → PatientInvoiceDetail → PatientBill` whose status equals `'UNPAID'` OR `'VERIFIED'`, THEN the request is rejected with HTTP 422 and the verbatim error message `'Could not get discharge summary. Patient have uncleared bills.'`; the gate applies to ALL admission payment types (CASH, INSURANCE, etc.).

GIVEN the bills-cleared gate passes (no UNPAID or VERIFIED bills on the admission), WHEN `approveDischargePlan` executes, THEN: (a) ALL `PatientInvoice` rows linked to the admission have their `status` set to `'APPROVED'`; (b) IF and ONLY IF `admission.status == 'STOPPED'`: `Admission.status → 'SIGNED-OUT'`, `Admission.dischargedBy` = plan.createdBy, `Admission.dischargedOn` = plan.createdOn, `Admission.dischargedAt` = plan.createdAt; `WardBed.status → 'EMPTY'`; `Patient.type → 'OUTPATIENT'`, `Patient.paymentType → 'CASH'`, `Patient.insurancePlan → null`; (c) `DischargePlan.status → 'APPROVED'`, `approvedBy = createdBy`, `approvedOn = createdOn`, `approvedAt = serverTimestamp`; (d) NO second-actor check is performed; (e) the approval is recorded via `AuditRecorder`.

GIVEN a `ReferralPlan` entity in the `inpatient` module, WHEN the schema is inspected, THEN the `referral_plans` table (pre-existing from V28) has `external_medical_provider_uid VARCHAR(26) NOT NULL` (mandatory loose ref, no FK), `admission_uid VARCHAR(26)` (nullable loose ref), `consultation_id BIGINT` (nullable real FK), a CHECK enforcing `num_nonnulls(consultation_id, admission_uid) = 1`, and status CHECK ('PENDING','APPROVED','ARCHIVED'); the inpatient module maps to the SAME table and SAME column as the V28 clinical migration.

GIVEN a `POST /api/v1/inpatient/referral-plans` request, WHEN BOTH `admissionUid` and `consultationUid` are provided OR WHEN NEITHER is provided, THEN the request is rejected with HTTP 422 and message `'Could not process. Patient can be an inpatient or outpatient but not both'` OR `'Could not process. Patient should either be an inpatient or outpatient'` respectively.

GIVEN a `POST /api/v1/inpatient/referral-plans` request with a valid `admissionUid` and a valid `externalMedicalProviderUid` (uid of an existing provider row), WHEN no existing PENDING plan exists for the admission, THEN: (a) `Admission.status → 'STOPPED'`; (b) a new `ReferralPlan` with `status = PENDING` is created, linking `admission_uid` and `external_medical_provider_uid`; (c) narrative fields copied from the request body.

GIVEN a `POST /api/v1/inpatient/referral-plans` request where `externalMedicalProviderUid` is blank or null, WHEN the service validates it, THEN the request is rejected with HTTP 422 (Bean Validation @NotBlank) — the `external_medical_provider_uid` column is NOT NULL and the Java field is annotated `@NotBlank`.

GIVEN a `GET /api/v1/inpatient/referral-plans/{uid}/approve` request for a ReferralPlan linked to an admission, WHEN ANY PatientInvoice→PatientInvoiceDetail→PatientBill for that admission has status 'UNPAID' OR 'VERIFIED', THEN reject with HTTP 422 and verbatim message `'Could not get discharge summary. Patient have uncleared bills.'`.
acceptanceCriteria.decisionRef: Q-disposition (bills-cleared gate on referral inpatient path); 03-DECISIONS-RATIFIED.md

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: GIVEN the bills-cleared gate passes on the referral inpatient approve path, WHEN `approveReferralPlan` executes, THEN: (a) all admission invoices set to 'APPROVED'; (b) IF admission.status == 'STOPPED': Admission → 'SIGNED-OUT', dischargedBy/On/At copied from plan.createdBy/On/At, WardBed → 'EMPTY', Patient.type → 'OUTPATIENT'; (c) ReferralPlan.status → 'APPROVED', approvedBy = createdBy, approvedOn = createdOn, approvedAt = serverTimestamp; (d) approval recorded via AuditRecorder.

GIVEN a `POST /api/v1/outpatient/referral-plans` request with a valid `consultationUid` (outpatient path), WHEN no existing PENDING plan exists for the consultation, THEN: (a) per-bill-type UNPAID checks are run over LabTest, Radiology, Procedure, Prescription bills — any UNPAID rejects with the appropriate verbatim message; (b) on pass: Consultation.status → 'SIGNED-OUT', Patient.paymentType → 'CASH', Patient.insurancePlan → null; (c) ReferralPlan created with status PENDING, consultation_id linked.

GIVEN a `DeceasedNote` entity in the `inpatient` module, WHEN the schema is inspected, THEN the `deceased_notes` table (pre-existing from V28) has `patient_summary TEXT` (nullable in DDL; REST enforces non-empty), `cause_of_death TEXT` (nullable in DDL; REST enforces non-empty), `death_date DATE` (mapped to Java `LocalDate`, column `date_`), `death_time TIME` (mapped to Java `LocalTime`, column `time_`); no `time_of_death TIMESTAMPTZ` column exists.

GIVEN a `POST /api/v1/inpatient/deceased-notes` request where `patientSummary` is blank OR `causeOfDeath` is blank, WHEN the service validates, THEN reject with HTTP 422 and verbatim message `'Summary and cause of death are missing'`.

GIVEN a `POST /api/v1/inpatient/deceased-notes` request with a valid `admissionUid` and non-blank `patientSummary` + `causeOfDeath`, WHEN no existing DeceasedNote exists for that admission AND the admission status is `'IN-PROCESS'`, THEN: (a) `Admission.status → 'HELD'`; (b) `WardBed.status → 'EMPTY'` (freed immediately at the HELD transition, BEFORE approve); (c) new DeceasedNote created with status PENDING; (d) `date_` and `time_` are copied verbatim from the request body — server clock is NOT substituted; (e) the final AdmissionBed row is left OPENED (no AdmissionBed.closedAt set here).
acceptanceCriteria.decisionRef: Q5 (deceased terminal via existing event); Q10 (AdmissionBed leak); 03-DECISIONS-RATIFIED.md

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: GIVEN a `POST /api/v1/inpatient/deceased-notes` request where admission.status is null (defensive case), WHEN the create logic executes, THEN: (a) admission.status is first set to 'IN-PROCESS' and persisted; (b) then set to 'HELD' with the WardBed freed to 'EMPTY'; (c) this reproduces the legacy null-status defensive branch verbatim.

GIVEN a `POST /api/v1/outpatient/deceased-notes` request with a valid `consultationUid` and non-blank `patientSummary` + `causeOfDeath`, WHEN no existing DeceasedNote exists for that consultation, THEN: (a) Consultation.status → 'HELD'; (b) new DeceasedNote created with status PENDING; (c) date_ and time_ copied verbatim from request body.

GIVEN a `GET /api/v1/inpatient/deceased-notes/{uid}/approve` request for a DeceasedNote linked to an admission, WHEN ANY PatientInvoice→PatientInvoiceDetail→PatientBill for that admission has status 'UNPAID' OR 'VERIFIED', THEN reject with HTTP 422 and verbatim message `'Could not get deceased summary. Patient have uncleared bills.'`; the gate checks BOTH admission invoices and (when present) consultation invoices.

GIVEN the bills-cleared gate passes on the deceased inpatient approve path, WHEN `approveDeceasedNote` executes, THEN: (a) all admission invoices set to 'APPROVED'; (b) IF admission.status == 'HELD': Admission.status → 'SIGNED-OUT', WardBed.status → 'EMPTY'; Patient.type → 'DECEASED' (via in-tx publish of the EXISTING `PatientDeceasedEvent` — the registration-side `PatientClosureListener.onPatientDeceased` flips the type BEFORE_COMMIT); (c) `Admission.dischargedBy/On/At` are NOT set (deceased path omits them); (d) DeceasedNote.status → 'APPROVED', approvedBy = createdBy, approvedOn = createdOn, approvedAt = serverTimestamp; (e) approval recorded via AuditRecorder; (f) the final AdmissionBed is left OPENED (verbatim leak).
acceptanceCriteria.decisionRef: Q5 + Q6 (PatientDeceasedEvent reuse; no new event/listener); Q10 (AdmissionBed leak); 03-DECISIONS-RATIFIED.md

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: GIVEN the deceased outpatient approve path (consultation-linked DeceasedNote), WHEN the bills-cleared gate passes, THEN: (a) Consultation.status → 'SIGNED-OUT'; Patient.type → 'DECEASED' via PatientDeceasedEvent publish; (b) DeceasedNote → 'APPROVED', approvedBy = createdBy, approvedOn = createdOn; (c) NO Admission.dischargedBy/On/At stamped.

GIVEN an inpatient deceased-note approval, WHEN `PatientDeceasedEvent(patientUid, actorUsername)` is published in-transaction by the `inpatient` service, THEN the registration-side `PatientClosureListener.onPatientDeceased` (annotated `@TransactionalEventListener(BEFORE_COMMIT)`) receives the event and calls `patient.changeType(PatientType.DECEASED)` — NO change to `PatientClosureListener.java` is required, and the `inpatient` module does NOT import registration types directly (event via `shared.event`).
acceptanceCriteria.decisionRef: Q6 (no new event/listener/field; no inpatient->registration compile edge); 03-DECISIONS-RATIFIED.md

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: GIVEN the HMSCLEAN2 inpatient module, WHEN `DischargePlan.approvedBy` is set on finalization, THEN `approvedBy == createdBy` (same ULID/username), `approvedOn == createdOn` (same business-day uid), and `approvedAt` = current server timestamp — no `approvedBy != createdBy` assertion or exception is thrown; `SELF_APPROVAL_FORBIDDEN` ErrorCode does NOT exist in `ErrorCode.java`.

GIVEN the HMSCLEAN2 inpatient module, WHEN `ReferralPlan.approvedBy` or `DeceasedNote.approvedBy` is set on finalization, THEN `approvedBy == createdBy` and `approvedOn == createdOn` in ALL disposition paths (discharge, referral-inpatient, referral-outpatient, deceased-inpatient, deceased-outpatient).

GIVEN insurance-covered bills (PatientBill.status = 'COVERED'), WHEN the bills-cleared gate iterates admission invoices at discharge/referral/deceased approve, THEN 'COVERED' bills do NOT trigger the rejection — only 'UNPAID' and 'VERIFIED' statuses block the gate.

GIVEN a terminal discharge (SIGNED-OUT via DischargePlan or ReferralPlan approve), WHEN `WardBed.status` is set to 'EMPTY', THEN the `AdmissionBed` record associated with the final `AdmissionBed.status = 'OPENED'` row is left OPENED (closedAt remains null) — no `AdmissionBed.closedAt` is written at discharge; data-migration note: expect dangling OPENED rows.

GIVEN the `GET /api/v1/inpatient/discharge-plans/list` endpoint, WHEN called, THEN it returns DischargePlans with status IN ('PENDING','APPROVED') — both statuses included, matching the legacy cashier-lane worklist; PENDING-only filter is NOT applied (that is tied to the phantom second-approver path).
acceptanceCriteria.decisionRef: Q4 (DEVIATION_NEEDS_CR; PENDING-only approver-worklist is the phantom); 03-DECISIONS-RATIFIED.md

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: GIVEN the `GET /api/v1/inpatient/referral-plans/list` and `GET /api/v1/inpatient/deceased-notes/list` endpoints, WHEN called, THEN each returns plans/notes with status IN ('PENDING','APPROVED') — ARCHIVED items are excluded from the list view.

GIVEN all three approve paths (discharge, referral, deceased), WHEN the approval succeeds, THEN an `AuditRecorder` record is appended in the same transaction (SHA-256 chained, actor from SecurityContext, action = APPROVE) — Hibernate Envers is NOT used.
acceptanceCriteria.legacyCite: shared/audit/AuditRecorder (ADR-0007); MEMORY: phantom Envers dep confirmed
acceptanceCriteria.decisionRef: Q4 (AuditRecorder mandatory for approvals); ADR-0007 (as cited in 03-DECISIONS-RATIFIED.md)

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: GIVEN a `POST /api/v1/inpatient/deceased-notes` or `POST /api/v1/inpatient/referral-plans` request, WHEN BOTH `admissionUid` and `consultationUid` are non-null, THEN reject with HTTP 422 and the verbatim XOR error message; WHEN BOTH are null, reject with the equivalent XOR null error message.

GIVEN a Testcontainers integration test for section 07a, WHEN a DischargePlan approve request is submitted for an admission with at least one PatientBill in status 'UNPAID', THEN the test ASSERTS HTTP 422 and the verbatim message; WHEN all bills are COVERED or PAID, the test ASSERTS HTTP 200 and Admission.status == 'SIGNED-OUT' and WardBed.status == 'EMPTY'.

GIVEN a Testcontainers integration test for the deceased inpatient path, WHEN `saveDeceasedNote` is called for an IN-PROCESS admission, THEN the test ASSERTS (a) Admission.status == 'HELD'; (b) WardBed.status == 'EMPTY' (freed at HELD, before approve); (c) final AdmissionBed has closedAt IS NULL (OPENED leak). Then when `approveDeceasedNote` is called with all bills cleared, THEN the test ASSERTS (d) Admission.status == 'SIGNED-OUT'; (e) Patient.type == 'DECEASED'; (f) Admission.dischargedAt IS NULL.

GIVEN the `SELF_APPROVAL_FORBIDDEN` ErrorCode criterion from the planning doc (07-inpatient-nursing.md:68), WHEN the 07a build spec is reviewed, THEN this criterion is NOT present as a frozen AC — it is parked behind CR-07-SoD (owner gate: engagement-lead + security-architect); no `SELF_APPROVAL_FORBIDDEN` enum constant is added to `ErrorCode.java`.

check.reason: No charter-violating smuggling: I verified all 33 ACs against the legacy source (PatientResource.java 5253-5390 discharge, 5392-5691 referral, 5693-5934 deceased; DischargePlan.java, ReferralPlan.java, DeceasedNote.java) and V28__clinical_closure_deceased_referral.sql. The frozen baseline correctly carries NO DischargePlan.kind discriminator (three separate entities), NO WardTransfer/TRANSFERRED, NO second-approver/SoD/SELF_APPROVAL_FORBIDDEN as frozen (AC-22 asserts absence; AC-32 frozen:false → CR-07-SoD), NO admit-time deceased guard/PATIENT_DECEASED/Patient.deceased boolean (deceased terminal via the EXISTING PatientDeceasedEvent, Q6), NO STALE_ENTITY/409 or pessimistic lock as frozen (correctly in blockedItems → CR-07-Q3/ADR-0017), NO real ExternalMedicalProvider FK (loose mandatory uid per Q7, real FK → CR-INC07-Q7), NO MAR/Envers/device-fingerprint (AC-28 excludes Envers), NO close-AdmissionBed-at-discharge (leak reproduced AC-25/31; fix frozen:false AC-33 → CR-07-Q10). The modern @NotBlank/422 on the loose external_medical_provider_uid is the correct Q7 reproduction of the mandatory-provider RULE, but the AC should state this explicitly (mandatory-rule reproduced via @NotBlank on the loose uid; the legacy 404-on-missing-provider is NOT reproduced because no external_medical_providers table exists — that rides CR-INC07-Q7) rather than implying byte-identical rejection semantics.

AC-07A-13 (descriptive consistency): the criterion text references columns 'date_' and 'time_', but the as-built V28 columns are death_date DATE / death_time TIME (V28:38-40, with the legacy @Column date_/time_ noted only in comments). Reword to name the as-built columns death_date/death_time (mapped to Java LocalDate/LocalTime) and cite the legacy @Column(name="date_")/("time_") at DeceasedNote.java:43-46 as the source — the current mix risks a schema-assertion test checking for non-existent 'date_'/'time_' columns.

AC-07A-12 (omission, advisory→tighten): the section specs the outpatient referral SAVE path (5460-5516) and its bill-gate, but there is NO AC for the outpatient referral APPROVE path (getReferralSummary consultation branch, PatientResource.java:5633-5681), which re-runs the per-bill-type UNPAID gate, sets Consultation.status='SIGNED-OUT', Patient.type='OUTPATIENT', and copies approvedBy=createdBy. Add an explicit AC (mirroring AC-07A-20 for deceased-outpatient) so the outpatient referral terminal is covered by parity tests.

AC-07A-12 (precision): note that on the legacy outpatient referral SAVE path Patient.type is NOT changed (only paymentType→CASH, insurancePlan→null at 5504-5507); Patient.type→OUTPATIENT happens on the APPROVE path (5674). AC-12 is correct in not asserting type change at save — keep, but make the save-vs-approve split explicit to avoid a tester adding a type assertion at save.

AC-07A-22 cite verification (advisory): the SELF_APPROVAL_FORBIDDEN-absent assertion is correct, but reference the verified single-actor copy lines explicitly per path (discharge 5378-5381, referral inpatient 5627-5630, referral outpatient 5679-5681, deceased 5904-5907) so the negative assertion (no approvedBy!=createdBy check) is anchored to all five paths, not just discharge.

AC-07A-01 (clarify modernization basis): the status CHECK ('PENDING','APPROVED') and the UNIQUE-partial-index 'one plan per admission' are NET-NEW DB constraints (legacy MySQL has free-text String status and enforces one-per-admission only via the @OneToOne admission FK + findByAdmission reuse at 5264-5266). These are legitimate ADR-0014 data-model modernizations (consistent with V28's ck_..._status pattern), but the AC should label them as modern constraints faithful to the legacy invariant, not imply legacy had a CHECK — so a reviewer does not mistake them for an extracted legacy artefact.

section.section: 07b — Five nursing-chart entities + free-text dosing-note path (NO MAR)
section.overview:
## Inc-07b: Five Nursing-Chart Entities + Free-Text Dosing-Note Path

Sub-increment **07b** adds the nurse-facing charting surface for the inpatient module. It builds **four new JPA entities** in `inpatient.domain` (backed by Flyway migrations V44–V47) and **activates the write paths** for two entities that already exist in `clinical.domain` but had their write endpoints deferred to the admission increment. No net-new MAR/MedicationAdministration entity is built — that is parked as CR-07-MAR.

### What 07b builds

| # | Entity | Module | Table | Status |
|---|--------|--------|-------|--------|
| 1 | `PatientVital` | `clinical.domain` (V23) | `patient_vitals` | **EXISTS** — 07b adds the ADMISSION write-path endpoint and the 3-context upsert gate |
| 2 | `PatientNursingChart` | `inpatient.domain` (V44) | `patient_nursing_charts` | **NEW** |
| 3 | `PatientNursingCarePlan` | `inpatient.domain` (V44) | `patient_nursing_care_plans` | **NEW** |
| 4 | `PatientNursingProgressNote` | `inpatient.domain` (V44) | `patient_nursing_progress_notes` | **NEW** |
| 5 | `PatientDressingChart` | `inpatient.domain` (V44) | `patient_dressing_charts` | **NEW** |
| 6 | `PatientPrescriptionChart` | `clinical.domain` (V27) | `patient_prescription_charts` | **EXISTS** — 07b adds the inpatient write endpoint + 24h DELETE window |

### Legacy-accurate entity shapes

**PatientVital** (`PatientVital.java:33-66`): 11 free-text String vital fields (pressure, temperature, pulseRate, weight, height, bodyMassIndex, bodyMassIndexComment, bodySurfaceArea, saturationOxygen, respiratoryRate, description). Status is a free-text string cycling `EMPTY → PENDING → SUBMITTED` in legacy. **Note:** the as-built `PatientVitalStatus` enum (inc-05 C5) has `EMPTY / SUBMITTED / ARCHIVED` — the intermediate `PENDING` state was dropped in inc-05 (ARCHIVED = the copied-to-GeneralExamination terminal). 07b activates the admission context on the existing entity; the `PENDING` gap is a pre-existing inc-05 parity deviation, not 07b's scope. Save transitions to `SUBMITTED` (not `PENDING`). **Unique asymmetry: no patient FK and no nurse FK on PatientVital** (`PatientVital.java:33-66`); the other four charts have mandatory patient and nullable nurse FKs.

**PatientNursingChart** (`PatientNursingChart.java:38-76`): care-activity columns (feeding, changingPosition, bedBathing, randomBloodSugar, fullBloodSugar) + fluid-balance columns (drainageOutput, fluidIntake, urineOutput) — all nullable free-text `VARCHAR(500)`. No status column. Links: `@ManyToOne` consultation / nonConsultation / admission (all nullable), `@ManyToOne` patient (NOT NULL, updatable=false), `@ManyToOne` nurse (nullable). No FluidBalance or CareActivity sub-entity — these are columns on this single combined snapshot. Save is admission-only hard-reject (outpatient/outsider explicitly rejected).

**PatientNursingCarePlan** (`PatientNursingCarePlan.java:38-72`): four free-text TEXT columns: nursingDiagnosis, expectedOutcome, implementation, evaluation. No status, no lifecycle. Links identical to PatientNursingChart. Save is admission-only insert (no edit path).

**PatientNursingProgressNote** (`PatientNursingProgressNote.java:38-69`): single free-text `note` TEXT column. No kind/category enum. Links identical to PatientNursingChart. Save is admission-only insert.

**PatientDressingChart** (`PatientDressingChart.java:40-69`): a **billing record**. Fields: `qty` `NUMERIC(19,2)` (double→BigDecimal modernization, pre-approved), `paymentType` VARCHAR(50) free-text, `membershipNo` VARCHAR(100). Mandatory `@OneToOne` patientBill (NOT NULL), mandatory `@ManyToOne` procedureType (NOT NULL). Links: nullable consultation / nonConsultation / admissionUid (loose ref), nullable nurse, nullable insurancePlan, `@ManyToOne` patient (NOT NULL). **No WoundStatus enum, no wound-description column.** The `Dressing` masterdata entity (table `dressings`) is a simple `@OneToOne` pointer to `ProcedureType` with no clinical fields (`Dressing.java:35-49`) — a marker that flags a ProcedureType as a billable dressing. Save triggers the 3-way billing side-effect (see billing ACs below). Delete triggers credit-note path.

**PatientPrescriptionChart** (`PatientPrescriptionChart.java:34-82`): entity and schema already exist (`clinical.domain`, V27). Fields: dosage, output, remark (all free-text String). Mandatory prescription FK. Loose admissionUid, clinicianUserUid, nurseUid. 07b adds the write endpoint. No MAR/administered-at/routeUid/patientResponse.

### Cross-cutting rules for all nursing saves (PatientServiceImpl.java:2593-2751, 2622-2635)
- Require exactly one context (consultation / nonConsultation / admission); hard-reject combinations and empty.
- For chart/note/care-plan/dressing: hard-reject consultation ('Operation not available for outpatients') and nonConsultation ('Operation not available for outsiders') — **admission-only in practice**.
- For vitals: accept all three context types (NOT admission-only).
- Require nurse present for chart/note/care-plan/dressing saves ('Nurse information not found').
- Admission status gate (PatientServiceImpl.java:2622-2635): `PENDING` → reject 'Could not be done. Admission not verified'; `IN-PROCESS` → proceed; any other status → reject 'Could not be done. Patient already signed off'.
- 24h deletion window (PatientResource.java:3135-3138): `ChronoUnit.HOURS.between(createdAt, now) >= 24` → reject 'Could not delete record. only records not exceeding 24 hours can be deleted'. No edit/update path on any of the four new entities.

### DressingChart billing side-effects (PatientServiceImpl.java:2078-2245)
Create path: always create PatientBill (billItem='Procedure', description='Dressing: '+name, amount=ProcedureType.price, status=UNPAID). Then:
- If `patient.paymentType == 'INSURANCE'` OR admission present → look up ProcedureTypeInsurancePlan(covered=true); if found: bill amount=paid=planPrice, balance=0, status=COVERED/INSURANCE + PatientInvoiceDetail under a PENDING PatientInvoice.
- Else if admission present: bill status=VERIFIED, attached to PENDING null-plan PatientInvoice.
Delete path (PatientResource.java:2967-3179, 3135-3138): 24h window guard; if a RECEIVED PatientPaymentDetail exists → raise PENDING PatientCreditNote; delete PatientInvoiceDetail (and PatientInvoice if empty); delete PatientPaymentDetail; delete chart + PatientBill.

### PatientPrescriptionChart write path (PatientServiceImpl.java:2533-2590)
- Nurse required ('Nurse information not found').
- Prescription.status must equal 'GIVEN' (pharmacy-dispensed) else reject 'Prescription not picked from pharmacy' (PatientServiceImpl.java:2544).
- Outpatient/outsider explicitly rejected ('Operation not available for outpatients' / 'Operation not available for outsiders') (PatientServiceImpl.java:2564-2569).
- Admission.status must be IN-PROCESS (PatientServiceImpl.java:2570-2577).
- Pure note write (dosage/output/remark). No stock movement, no charge, no Prescription mutation.
- DELETE within 24h only (PatientResource.java:3109-3121). No edit path.

### What is NOT built in 07b (parked CRs)
- **CR-07-MAR**: no `MedicationAdministration` entity, no `route`, no `patientResponse`, no `administeredAt` distinct from `createdAt`, no status on prescription chart.
- **CR-07-Q11** (consumable quirks) are in 07c scope.
- **Q8 / CR-07-ward-transfer**: `TRANSFERRED` admission state and WardTransfer entity excluded entirely.
- PatientVital PENDING state (pre-existing inc-05 gap, not a 07b CR).

### Flyway migrations
- **V44**: `patient_nursing_charts`, `patient_nursing_care_plans`, `patient_nursing_progress_notes`
- **V45**: `patient_dressing_charts`
- (PatientVital and PatientPrescriptionChart tables already exist in V23/V27; no schema changes needed for their 07b write paths)

### API endpoints (07b surface, all under inpatient module)
Vitals (re-uses clinical entity — 07b adds admission context):
- `POST /inpatient/admissions/uid/{admissionUid}/vitals` — upsert (last-wins) + transition to SUBMITTED
- `POST /inpatient/admissions/uid/{admissionUid}/vitals/submit` — SUBMITTED→ (no-op if already SUBMITTED; rejects if already SUBMITTED per legacy)
- `GET  /inpatient/admissions/uid/{admissionUid}/vitals` — load

Nursing charts:
- `POST   /inpatient/admissions/uid/{admissionUid}/nursing-charts` — insert
- `GET    /inpatient/admissions/uid/{admissionUid}/nursing-charts` — list all
- `DELETE /inpatient/admissions/uid/{admissionUid}/nursing-charts/uid/{uid}` — 24h window

Care plans:
- `POST   /inpatient/admissions/uid/{admissionUid}/care-plans`
- `GET    /inpatient/admissions/uid/{admissionUid}/care-plans`
- `DELETE /inpatient/admissions/uid/{admissionUid}/care-plans/uid/{uid}`

Progress notes:
- `POST   /inpatient/admissions/uid/{admissionUid}/progress-notes`
- `GET    /inpatient/admissions/uid/{admissionUid}/progress-notes`
- `DELETE /inpatient/admissions/uid/{admissionUid}/progress-notes/uid/{uid}`

Dressing charts:
- `POST   /inpatient/admissions/uid/{admissionUid}/dressing-charts`
- `GET    /inpatient/admissions/uid/{admissionUid}/dressing-charts`
- `DELETE /inpatient/admissions/uid/{admissionUid}/dressing-charts/uid/{uid}`

Prescription dosing notes (free-text only, NO MAR):
- `POST   /inpatient/admissions/uid/{admissionUid}/prescription-charts`
- `GET    /inpatient/admissions/uid/{admissionUid}/prescription-charts`
- `DELETE /inpatient/admissions/uid/{admissionUid}/prescription-charts/uid/{uid}`

All endpoints: `authenticated()` (ADR-0006 deny-by-default hardening, net-new — legacy was ungated). ADMISSION-CREATE is the only legacy-valid privilege code; all other privilege codes are net-new RBAC hardening.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Given the PatientVital entity already exists in clinical.domain (V23 schema, PatientVitalStatus EMPTY/SUBMITTED/ARCHIVED), when 07b activates the ADMISSION write path, then POST /inpatient/admissions/uid/{admissionUid}/vitals resolves the admission loose-uid, applies the context-exclusivity gate (exactly one of consultation/nonConsultation/admissionUid non-null; PatientResource.java:1028-1071), finds the last PatientVital row by admissionUid (last-wins upsert pattern; PatientResource.java:1043-1114), overwrites all 11 vital fields in-place if found or inserts a new row, and transitions status to SUBMITTED (the as-built inc-05 enum value mapping PENDING→SUBMITTED; PatientVital.java:45).

Given an existing PatientVital row for the admission with status SUBMITTED, when POST /inpatient/admissions/uid/{admissionUid}/vitals is called again, then the service rejects with HTTP 422 and message 'Could not modify vitals. Vitals already submitted' (PatientResource.java:1074-1076); no field overwrites occur.

Given a request body with more than one of {admissionUid, consultationUid, nonConsultationUid} non-null, when POST /vitals is called on any context path, then the service rejects 422 with message 'Patient can not have admission and consultation and outsider simultaneously' (PatientResource.java:1028-1071); exactly one context must be present.

Given a request body where all three context ids are empty/null, when POST /vitals is called, then the service rejects 422 with message 'No Admission or Consultation or Non Consultation found' (PatientResource.java:1028-1071).

Given a GET /inpatient/admissions/uid/{admissionUid}/vitals request, when no PatientVital row exists for the admission, then the endpoint auto-creates a row with status=EMPTY and all vital fields null (side-effecting GET, PatientResource.java:1298-1307 legacy pattern).

Given that PatientVital has NO patient FK and NO nurse FK (PatientVital.java:33-66 — unique asymmetry vs the four nursing charts), when a PatientVital row is created or updated, then no patient_uid and no nurse_uid are written to the row; only the context binding (admissionUid) and audit columns are persisted.

Given a new PatientNursingChart entity in inpatient.domain backed by V44 table patient_nursing_charts, when the entity is created, then it contains exactly the following columns: feeding VARCHAR(500), changing_position VARCHAR(500), bed_bathing VARCHAR(500), random_blood_sugar VARCHAR(500), full_blood_sugar VARCHAR(500), drainage_output VARCHAR(500), fluid_intake VARCHAR(500), urine_output VARCHAR(500) — all nullable free-text Strings, NO status column, NO FluidBalance or CareActivity sub-entity (PatientNursingChart.java:38-45).

Given a POST /inpatient/admissions/uid/{admissionUid}/nursing-charts request with a nurse uid, when the admission status is IN-PROCESS, then the service inserts a new PatientNursingChart row (append-only, no upsert), stamps createdAt/createdBy, and links admission, patient, and nurse (PatientServiceImpl.java:2593-2751).

Given a POST /inpatient/admissions/uid/{admissionUid}/nursing-charts request without a nurse uid in the security context / request, when the service evaluates the nurse guard, then it rejects 422 with 'Nurse information not found' (PatientServiceImpl.java:2593-2751).

Given a POST /inpatient/admissions/uid/{admissionUid}/nursing-charts request where the admission status is PENDING, when the service evaluates the status gate, then it rejects 422 with 'Could not be done. Admission not verified' (PatientServiceImpl.java:2622-2635).

Given a POST /inpatient/admissions/uid/{admissionUid}/nursing-charts request where the admission status is STOPPED, HELD, or SIGNED-OUT, when the service evaluates the status gate, then it rejects 422 with 'Could not be done. Patient already signed off' (PatientServiceImpl.java:2622-2635).

Given an existing PatientNursingChart row whose createdAt is less than 24 hours ago, when DELETE /inpatient/admissions/uid/{admissionUid}/nursing-charts/uid/{uid} is called, then the service hard-deletes the row (repository.delete) with no billing reversal and returns HTTP 200 (PatientResource.java:3135-3138).

Given an existing PatientNursingChart row whose createdAt is 24 or more hours ago (ChronoUnit.HOURS.between(createdAt, now) >= 24), when DELETE is called on it, then the service rejects 422 with 'Could not delete record. only records not exceeding 24 hours can be deleted' (PatientResource.java:3135-3138).

Given a POST /inpatient/admissions/.../nursing-charts using a consultation context (not an admission), when the service evaluates the context gate, then it rejects 422 with 'Operation not available for outpatients'; and when a nonConsultation context is used, it rejects 422 with 'Operation not available for outsiders' (PatientServiceImpl.java:2593-2751).

Given a new PatientNursingCarePlan entity in inpatient.domain backed by V44 table patient_nursing_care_plans, when the entity is created, then it contains exactly four TEXT columns: nursing_diagnosis, expected_outcome, implementation, evaluation — all nullable free-text Strings, NO status column, NO ACTIVE/RESOLVED/CANCELLED lifecycle (PatientNursingCarePlan.java:38-41).

Given a POST /inpatient/admissions/uid/{admissionUid}/care-plans request with nurse, valid IN-PROCESS admission, then the service inserts a new PatientNursingCarePlan row (append-only insert), links admission + patient + nurse, stamps audit fields. Identical admission-status gate (PENDING→'not verified'; signed-off→'already signed off') and nurse guard ('Nurse information not found') as PatientNursingChart apply verbatim (PatientServiceImpl.java:2593-2751, 2622-2635).

Given a PatientNursingCarePlan row less than 24h old, when DELETE is called, then it is hard-deleted with no billing reversal; when 24h or more old, then 422 'Could not delete record. only records not exceeding 24 hours can be deleted' (PatientResource.java:3135-3138).

Given a new PatientNursingProgressNote entity in inpatient.domain backed by V44 table patient_nursing_progress_notes, when the entity is created, then it contains exactly ONE TEXT column: note — nullable free-text String, NO kind/category enum, NO typed DOCTOR/NURSING/OBSERVATION/HANDOVER column (PatientNursingProgressNote.java:38).

Given a POST /inpatient/admissions/uid/{admissionUid}/progress-notes request with nurse, valid IN-PROCESS admission, then the service inserts a new PatientNursingProgressNote row (append-only insert), links admission + patient + nurse, stamps audit fields. Identical status gate and nurse guard apply (PatientServiceImpl.java:2593-2751, 2622-2635).

Given a PatientNursingProgressNote row, when DELETE is called within 24h then hard-delete; when 24h or more old then 422 with the verbatim rejection message (PatientResource.java:3135-3138). No edit path exists.

Given a new PatientDressingChart entity in inpatient.domain backed by V45 table patient_dressing_charts, when the entity is created, then it contains: qty NUMERIC(19,2) NOT NULL, payment_type VARCHAR(50), membership_no VARCHAR(100), mandatory @OneToOne patient_bill_id (NOT NULL), mandatory @ManyToOne procedure_type_id (NOT NULL), nullable admission_uid VARCHAR(26) loose ref, nullable nurse_id, nullable insurance_plan_id, @ManyToOne patient_id (NOT NULL). NO wound_status column, NO wound_description column (PatientDressingChart.java:40-69).

Given a POST /inpatient/admissions/uid/{admissionUid}/dressing-charts request, when the ProcedureType is not registered in the dressings masterdata table (no Dressing row with procedureType matching the request), then the service rejects 404 with 'Procedure type is not listed as dressing' (Dressing.java:35-49).

Given a valid POST /inpatient/admissions/uid/{admissionUid}/dressing-charts request with patient.paymentType=CASH and no insurance, when the service processes the create, then it creates a PatientBill with billItem='Procedure', description='Dressing: <procedureType.name>', amount=ProcedureType.price, qty=1 (legacy literal, not the chart qty), status=UNPAID (PatientServiceImpl.java:2078-2245).

Given a valid POST request where patient.paymentType=INSURANCE and a ProcedureTypeInsurancePlan(covered=true) exists for the plan, when the service processes the create, then the PatientBill is set status=COVERED/INSURANCE with amount=paid=planPrice, balance=0, and a PatientInvoiceDetail is added under a PENDING PatientInvoice (creating the invoice if none exists for the admission); the active-flag on the plan is NOT checked (insurance-quirk inherited from general billing pattern; PatientServiceImpl.java:2078-2245).

Given a valid POST request with admission present and no covered ProcedureTypeInsurancePlan (cash-on-admission path), when the service processes the create, then the PatientBill is set status=VERIFIED, attached to a PENDING null-plan PatientInvoice via PatientInvoiceDetail (PatientServiceImpl.java:2396-2465 pattern applied to dressing).

Given an existing PatientDressingChart row less than 24h old where a RECEIVED PatientPaymentDetail exists for the linked PatientBill, when DELETE /dressing-charts/uid/{uid} is called, then the service: (1) raises a PENDING PatientCreditNote; (2) deletes the PatientInvoiceDetail (and the parent PatientInvoice if it becomes empty); (3) deletes the PatientPaymentDetail; (4) deletes the PatientDressingChart; (5) deletes the PatientBill. No stock is restored (billing-only baseline, PatientResource.java:2967-3179).

Given an existing PatientDressingChart row 24h or more old, when DELETE is called, then the service rejects 422 with 'Could not delete record. only records not exceeding 24 hours can be deleted'; no reversal or deletion occurs (PatientResource.java:3135-3138).

Given a POST /dressing-charts request with admission status PENDING, when the service evaluates the admission gate, then it rejects 422 'Could not be done. Admission not verified'; with IN-PROCESS it proceeds; with STOPPED/HELD/SIGNED-OUT it rejects 422 'Could not be done. Patient already signed off' (PatientServiceImpl.java:2062-2069, 2622-2635).

Given the PatientPrescriptionChart entity already exists in clinical.domain (V27 table patient_prescription_charts), when 07b activates the write path, then POST /inpatient/admissions/uid/{admissionUid}/prescription-charts (a) resolves the linked Prescription by uid, (b) rejects 422 'Prescription not picked from pharmacy' if Prescription.status != 'GIVEN' (PatientServiceImpl.java:2544), (c) rejects 422 'Operation not available for outpatients' / 'Operation not available for outsiders' if the request carries a consultation / nonConsultation binding (PatientServiceImpl.java:2564-2569), and (d) rejects 422 'Could not be done. Admission not verified' if admission.status == PENDING (PatientServiceImpl.java:2570-2577).

Given a valid POST /inpatient/admissions/uid/{admissionUid}/prescription-charts request where a nurse uid is present, admission status is IN-PROCESS, and the linked Prescription.status == 'GIVEN', when the service processes the create, then it stamps nurseUid, clinicianUserUid (optional), businessDayUid, admissionUid on the new PatientPrescriptionChart row, saves it, and returns HTTP 201. No stock movement and no Prescription mutation occur (PatientServiceImpl.java:2533-2590).

Given a POST /inpatient/admissions/.../prescription-charts request with no nurse uid in the security context, when the service evaluates the nurse guard, then it rejects 422 with 'Nurse information not found' (PatientServiceImpl.java:2536-2538).

Given an existing PatientPrescriptionChart row less than 24h old, when DELETE /inpatient/admissions/uid/{admissionUid}/prescription-charts/uid/{uid} is called, then the service physically deletes the row (repository.delete). When the row is 24h or more old, the service rejects 422 'Could not delete record. only records not exceeding 24 hours can be deleted' (PatientResource.java:3109-3121). No edit/update path exists.

Given the PatientPrescriptionChart entity has fields dosage (VARCHAR 200), output (VARCHAR 200), remark (TEXT) — all free-text String, NO status field, NO administeredAt distinct from createdAt, NO routeUid, NO patientResponse (PatientPrescriptionChart.java:38-40), when a chart row is created, then no MAR-type structured fields exist on the persisted entity.

BLOCKED — CR-07-MAR (parked): Given a MedicationAdministration entity is requested with per-dose administration events (administeredAt, doseGiven, routeUid, patientResponse, administeredByUsername), when the build spec is evaluated, then this entity MUST NOT be built in 07b baseline. Building it requires engagement-lead + HDE approval of CR-07-MAR and a ratified IAM vocabulary extension for route references.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Given every nursing chart create/delete operation is a clinical write, when any of the five chart entities (PatientNursingChart, PatientNursingCarePlan, PatientNursingProgressNote, PatientDressingChart) or PatientPrescriptionChart is created or deleted, then AuditRecorder.record() is called with the entity uid, actor from SecurityContext, action (CREATE or DELETE), and the SHA-256 chained checksum (ADR-0007). No Hibernate Envers @Audited annotation is used (confirmed phantom dep — zero @Audited across com.orbix.api).
acceptanceCriteria.legacyCite: n/a — modern data-type only (AuditRecorder ADR-0007 net-new; legacy had only createdBy/createdOn/createdAt manual stamps)
acceptanceCriteria.decisionRef: ADR-0007 (AuditRecorder net-new capability; not legacy parity)

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Given migrations V1–V43 are already applied (inc-08 ended at V43), when 07b Flyway migrations run, then V44 creates tables patient_nursing_charts, patient_nursing_care_plans, patient_nursing_progress_notes (all with BIGINT hidden id + ULID uid NOT NULL UNIQUE, audit columns, and the loose-ref FK columns), V45 creates patient_dressing_charts with the mandatory patient_bill_id and procedure_type_id FKs; both scripts are idempotent (Flyway checksum). No V23 or V27 changes are needed as PatientVital and PatientPrescriptionChart tables already exist.

Given the legacy inpatient nursing endpoints were entirely ungated (PatientResource.java:2135,2152,2169,2186,2203,2220,2237 — no @PreAuthorize, all commented out), when 07b REST controllers are built, then all nursing chart endpoints are secured with Spring Security authenticated() (ADR-0006 deny-by-default hardening, net-new). The ADMISSION-CREATE privilege code (legacy-valid, Object_.java:25 — ADMISSION-ALL CREATE READ UPDATE) may optionally gate the dressing-chart POST. All other privilege codes are net-new RBAC hardening NOT reproducible from legacy and must NOT be asserted as legacy-parity criteria.
acceptanceCriteria.decisionRef: ADR-0006 (deny-by-default hardening, net-new); Q4 ratified (single-actor, no SoD gate)

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Given that PatientPrescriptionChart has no clinical::api read surface (only the package-private PatientPrescriptionChartRepository exists — PatientPrescriptionChart.java:20-24), when 07b implements GET /inpatient/admissions/uid/{admissionUid}/prescription-charts, then the read is served from the inpatient module's own query on the clinical domain table (via the inpatient module's Spring Data repository or a published clinical read port if the solution-architect confirms a cross-module read is needed). No new clinical::api publish is required for the basic read within the inpatient module.
acceptanceCriteria.legacyCite: PatientPrescriptionChart.java:20-24 (no clinical::api read surface — only package-private repo)

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Golden-master parity test: Given a test admission in IN-PROCESS state with a nurse, when the IT (Testcontainers + PostgreSQL 16) creates one PatientNursingChart row with feeding='porridge', one PatientNursingCarePlan row, one PatientNursingProgressNote row with note='Patient improving', and one PatientDressingChart row for a Dressing-registered ProcedureType, then (a) all four rows are retrievable via GET, (b) the dressing PatientBill exists with status=UNPAID and amount=ProcedureType.price, (c) all rows can be hard-deleted within the 24h window, and (d) after 24h-window expiry a DELETE attempt returns 422.

Golden-master parity test for PatientPrescriptionChart write path: Given an IN-PROCESS admission with a nurse and a Prescription with status='GIVEN', when POST /prescription-charts is called with dosage='10mg oral', output='nil', remark='patient tolerated well', then (a) a PatientPrescriptionChart row is created with those exact free-text values, nurseUid, and admissionUid, (b) no stock movement occurred, (c) no Prescription.status mutation occurred, (d) the row is deletable within 24h and rejected after 24h (PatientServiceImpl.java:2533-2590; PatientResource.java:3109-3121).

Golden-master parity test for PENDING admission rejection: Given a PENDING admission (ward-bed bill not yet paid), when POST to any nursing chart endpoint (nursing-charts, care-plans, progress-notes, dressing-charts, prescription-charts) is attempted, then every call returns HTTP 422 with the exact message 'Could not be done. Admission not verified' (PatientServiceImpl.java:2622-2635).
