check.section: 07b — Five nursing-chart entities + free-text dosing-note path (NO MAR)
check.reason: The section is ~85% sound: it correctly drops MAR, models five (not six) flat chart entities with free-text columns (no FluidBalance/CareActivity sub-entities, no NursingProgressNote.kind, no CarePlan lifecycle, no WoundStatus), uses DELETE-only with the 24h window (no edit path), reproduces the admission-status gate verbatim, parks MAR/SoD/PATIENT_DECEASED/STALE_ENTITY/WardTransfer correctly as frozen:false with the right gates, and uses AuditRecorder (not Envers). However it contains ONE blocking charter violation, several baseline-accuracy/cite defects, and a pervasive decisionRef misattribution that must be corrected before this can freeze. The blocking issue: AC-07B-DRS-03 freezes the dressing bill-line literals billItem='Procedure' / description='Dressing: <name>' as frozen:true, and the overview/blockedItems explicitly assert these are reproducible 'directly' and are NOT subject to CR-07-Q13-billing-display. This is false and identical in kind to the Q13 consumable trap the ratified register caught. The dressing PatientBill is a plan-priced UNPAID/COVERED/VERIFIED trichotomy with PatientInvoiceDetail accumulation, so it MUST flow through billing::api recordClinicalCharge(ChargeRequest) (ServiceKind.PROCEDURE) — the only flat-cash seam that carries billItem/description, recordFlatCashSale, is OTC-only against the GENERAL dummy patient with no plan engine and no invoice accumulator (verified BillingCommands.java:65-92, ChargeRequest.java:27-37). recordClinicalCharge derives billItem=labelFor(kind) and description=billItem (Q13 correction A/B), so a PROCEDURE-kind dressing charge emits billItem='Procedure'/description='Procedure', NOT 'Dressing: <name>'. Reproducing the legacy literal is the SAME verified display drift that requires a billing-API extension — it must be marked frozen:false behind a CR-07-Q13-billing-display sibling (or the Q13 gate broadened to cover dressing), exactly as the consumable literals are. Freezing it smuggles a billing-display deviation into the frozen baseline. Secondary defects (below) are corrections, not blockers, but several are cite/accuracy errors that would mislead the builder.
corrections: BLOCKING — AC-07B-DRS-03 smuggles billing-display drift: the dressing bill literals billItem='Procedure'/description='Dressing: <name>' are NOT reproducible through the as-built billing seam. The dressing bill is a plan-priced UNPAID/COVERED/VERIFIED trichotomy with PatientInvoiceDetail accumulation, so it must use billing::api recordClinicalCharge (ServiceKind.PROCEDURE), whose ChargeRequest (ChargeRequest.java:27-37) carries NO billItem/description and whose impl derives billItem=labelFor(kind)/description=billItem (Q13 corrections A/B) — emitting 'Procedure'/'Procedure', not 'Dressing: <name>'. recordFlatCashSale (BillingCommands.java:90-92) DOES carry billItem/description but is OTC-flat-cash-only against the GENERAL dummy patient with no plan engine/invoice accumulator and cannot serve a COVERED/VERIFIED dressing bill. Mark the literal-display portion of AC-07B-DRS-03 frozen:false behind a CR (the same CR-07-Q13-billing-display, or a named sibling), and DELETE the false claim in the overview and blockedItems that 'dressing uses Procedure/Dressing: <name> directly' / 'affects 07c consumable path, not 07b dressing path'. The amount (ProcedureType.price), qty=1, and status=UNPAID portions remain freezable.

BLOCKING-ADJACENT — AC-07B-VIT-01 misstates the legacy save-status transition as a frozen legacy-parity fact. Legacy save_patient_vitals sets status 'PENDING' on both create and update (discovery 00-raw line 420: PatientResource.java:1088,1107), and submit_patient_vitals is the step that sets SUBMITTED (PatientResource.java:1180-1188). The as-built inc-05 entity (PatientVital.java submitVitals() line 196-219) collapsed this two-step into a single save→SUBMITTED because inc-05 dropped the PENDING state. AC-07B-VIT-01 therefore reproduces the AS-BUILT inc-05 behaviour, NOT legacy — yet it is tagged frozen:true with a legacy cite (PatientResource.java:1043-1114) and claims a 'PENDING→SUBMITTED mapping'. Relabel: this AC inherits the pre-existing inc-05 PENDING-collapse deviation (already logged as CR-INC05-VITALS-PENDING in blockedItems); its frozen:true legacy-parity claim and the legacy save-status cite are inaccurate. Either restate it as 'reproduces the as-built inc-05 staging entity (which itself carries the CR-INC05-VITALS-PENDING gap)' or drop the legacy-parity framing.

AC-07B-VIT-05 (side-effecting GET auto-creates EMPTY row) is mis-scoped to admission. The legacy auto-create-on-GET is the consultation worklist path (PatientResource.java:1298-1307, loadPatientVitalByConsultationId — confirmed 00-raw line 420 and as-built PatientVital.java:48 javadoc). There is no evidence the admission-context GET auto-creates a row, and PatientVital uniquely has no admission auto-create cite. Either supply an admission-specific legacy cite or drop the auto-create-on-admission-GET assertion (do not generalise the consultation behaviour to admission absent a cite).

Pervasive decisionRef misattribution: ~18 of the 23 frozen ACs are tagged 'Q9-baseline (07b FREEZABLE)'. Q9 is the WARD-INSURANCE active-flag-ignored quirk + top-up split (07a) — it has nothing to do with nursing charts. The nursing-chart 07b scope derives from Q1 (PatientPrescriptionChart dosing-note path) and from the reconciliation doc's 07b scope synthesis (the five chart entities), NOT Q9. Re-tag all vitals/nursing-chart/care-plan/progress-note/dressing ACs to a correct reference (e.g. '07b scope, 01-RECONCILIATION line 87 / 00-discovery nursing-charts lane' for the four charts; 'Q1 ratified' for the prescription chart). Leaving Q9 as the authority for the entire 07b surface is a traceability defect that would not survive RTM review.

AC-07B-DRS-01 entity-shape cite range and field set are incomplete. The draft and its cites use PatientDressingChart.java:40-69, but the discovery ground truth reads the entity as PatientDressingChart.java:40-95 (00-raw lines 411, 534) — the billing fields extend past line 69. Also the legacy entity carries a nullable 'clinician' FK (00-raw line 410: 'Nullable FKs: consultation/nonConsultation/admission, clinician, nurse, insurancePlan') which AC-07B-DRS-01 omits. Correct the cite range to :40-95 and add the nullable clinician link to the column list.

Verify the 'Procedure type is not listed as dressing' HTTP status. AC-07B-DRS-02 asserts 404; the discovery extract does not state the legacy HTTP code (00-raw line 415 only gives the message). Most sibling not-found/guard rejections in this surface map to 422 BUSINESS_RULE. Either supply a legacy cite confirming 404 for this guard or align it to the project's guard-rejection convention; do not assert 404 as frozen on assumption.

AC-07B-PARITY-01(b) asserts the dressing PatientBill 'status=UNPAID' for the golden-master, but the dressing trichotomy makes an admission-context cash dressing VERIFIED (AC-07B-DRS-05), not UNPAID — UNPAID is the non-insurance non-admission branch (AC-07B-DRS-03). Since the parity test fixture is an IN-PROCESS admission, the expected status is VERIFIED (or COVERED if insured), not UNPAID. Fix the parity assertion to match the branch its own fixture selects, or change the fixture to a non-admission CASH context.

AC-07B-DRS-04 'active flag NOT checked' is correct behaviour but is justified as 'insurance-quirk inherited from general billing pattern' — note that the Q9 active-flag-ignored ratification is specifically about WardTypeInsurancePlan, not ProcedureTypeInsurancePlan. The dressing path reads ProcedureTypeInsurancePlan(covered=true). Keep the no-active-check behaviour (it matches the general covered-only lookup pattern) but cite the dressing-specific legacy lookup (PatientServiceImpl.java:2078-2245) rather than implying it rides the Q9 ward decision.

Advisory (non-blocking, consistent with Q1 challenge correction): the overview header and AC-07B-PXC-04 correctly state DELETE-only/no-edit, but ensure no AC anywhere implies an in-place 'edit'/'update' on any of the five entities under cover of 'consistency'. Legacy has save+delete only (00-raw line 496). The current draft is clean on this; keep it explicit in the parity tests.

Advisory: AC-07B-RBAC-01 correctly labels authenticated() as net-new ADR-0006 hardening (not parity) and ADMISSION-CREATE as the only legacy-valid code, and correctly avoids a 403-on-wrong-role parity assertion on the formerly-ungated surface. No change needed — confirming it passes the RBAC checks. Sub-increment placement (07b for the five charts + dosing-note path) is correct per the ratified register (07b FREEZABLE NOW).

section.section: 07c — Consumable Issue (Billing-Only) + Three Latent Bugs + Ward-Accrual Parity Oracle
section.overview:
## Inc-07c: Consumable Issue (Billing-Only) + Latent-Bug Reproduction + Ward-Accrual Parity Oracle

### Sub-increment identity
**07c** is the third and final fragment of the Inpatient & Nursing build. It adds two orthogonal surfaces:

1. **`savePatientConsumableChart` (billing-only baseline)** — reproduces `PatientServiceImpl.java:2250-2475` verbatim: guards, a three-way `PatientBill` status outcome (UNPAID cash / COVERED insured / VERIFIED cash-on-admission), and the `deleteConsumableChart` reversal path. No stock engine is touched (Q12 = billing-only). **Three latent bugs (Q11) are reproduced VERBATIM as the exact-process baseline.**

2. **`accrueWardDay` parity oracle** — an idempotent, unbound service method reproducing `UpdatePatient.java:258-334` for the ward-accrual total/status semantics. The method is callable via a manual `POST /ops/jobs/ward-accrual` trigger only; no `@Scheduled` or ShedLock binding is built (CR-07-Q2, ADR-0018 not ratified).

### Legacy-accurate behaviour

**Consumable issue:**
- Guard chain (in order): `Medicine` exists (404) → `qty > 0` ("Qty can not be zero") → `Consumable` masterdata row exists for that `Medicine` ("Medicine is not listed as consumable") → nurse uid present → exactly-one context (outpatient/outsider paths throw) → `Admission.status` == `IN-PROCESS` (PENDING → "Admission not verified"; else → "Patient already signed off") (`PatientServiceImpl.java:2252-2303`).
- Chart row: `PatientConsumableChart.status` hard-coded `"NOT-GIVEN"` on creation; never advanced (`domain/PatientConsumableChart.java:40-96; PatientServiceImpl.java:2305`). Status `"GIVEN"` is read-only delete guard only (`PatientResource.java:3035`).
- Three-way `PatientBill` outcome:
  - **UNPAID** (cash default): `amount = Medicine.price` (unit price only, **not price × qty**), `balance = price × qty`, `status = "UNPAID"` (`PatientServiceImpl.java:2307-2319`). Note: legacy cash `amount` is the unit price, not `price × qty` — this diverges from the as-built `computeAmount(MEDICINE)` which returns `price × qty`; must be golden-mastered at 2dp (frozen:false until Q13 owner confirms, see AC-07C-13).
  - **COVERED** (insurance, `MedicineInsurancePlan.covered=true`): `amount = paid = planPrice × qty`, `balance = 0`, `status = "COVERED"`, `paymentType = "INSURANCE"`; `PatientInvoiceDetail` appended to a `PENDING PatientInvoice` (created with `no='id'` if none exists) (`PatientServiceImpl.java:2321-2395`). **BUG Q11-1**: `PatientInvoiceDetail.qty` hard-coded to `1` (not `chart.qty`) at lines `:2372, :2388`.
  - **VERIFIED** (cash-on-admission, no covered plan): `amount = balance = price × qty`, `paid = 0`, `status = "VERIFIED"`, attached to a `PENDING` null-plan `PatientInvoice` (`PatientServiceImpl.java:2396-2465`). **BUG Q11-1**: `PatientInvoiceDetail.qty` hard-coded to `1` at lines `:2441, :2457`.
- Pricing kind: `ServiceKind.MEDICINE` via `PriceLookup.resolve(planUid, MEDICINE, medicineUid)` (Q13 — owner confirm pending; reproducing legacy `billItem='Medication'` / `description='Consumable: <name>'` requires CR-07-Q13-billing-display because `ChargeRequest.java:27-37` has no `billItem`/`description` fields and `BillingChargeService` emits `'Medicine'/'Medicine'` — mark bill-line literal criteria frozen:false).
- Chart + bill persisted last (`PatientServiceImpl.java:2467-2474`). No stock entity read or written anywhere in the method. **No `FEFO`, no negative-stock gate, no pessimistic lock.**

**Delete path (`PatientResource.java:3026-3085`):**
- **GIVEN guard**: if `chart.status == "GIVEN"` → reject "Consumable already given to patient" (`:3035`).
- **24h window**: `ChronoUnit.HOURS.between(chart.createdAt, now) >= 24` → reject (same 24h mechanic as other charts).
- **BUG Q11-2**: if a `RECEIVED` `PatientPaymentDetail` exists → raise a `PENDING PatientCreditNote` with `reference = "Canceled lab test"` (copy-paste from lab-delete path, `:3053`). Reproduced verbatim.
- **BUG Q11-3**: parent `PatientInvoice` cascade-delete: counter loop `int j = 0; for(PatientInvoiceDetail d : ...) { j = j++; }` — `j = j++` is a Java no-op, `j` is always 0, so the `if(j == 0)` guard fires UNCONDITIONALLY and the parent `PatientInvoice` is deleted regardless of whether other details remain (`PatientResource.java:3070-3076`). `PatientInvoice.java:80` declares `@OneToMany(orphanRemoval=true, fetch=EAGER)`, so deleting the parent cascade-wipes all sibling `PatientInvoiceDetail` rows. Reproduced verbatim; golden-master asserts that deleting one consumable chart from a shared multi-detail `PENDING` invoice removes the entire invoice and all its sibling lines.
- No stock is restored (none was decremented).

**Ward-accrual parity oracle (`accrueWardDay`):**
- For admissions in `{IN-PROCESS, STOPPED}`: find all `OPENED` `AdmissionBed` rows for the admission.
- **De-dup branch**: if `count(OPENED) > 1` → close ALL opened beds (`CLOSED` + `closedAt = now`) and return without creating a new charge that cycle (`UpdatePatient.java:267-283`).
- **Normal branch**: if exactly one `OPENED` bed and `ChronoUnit.HOURS.between(openedAt, now) >= 24` (`UpdatePatient.java:291-292`):
  1. Close existing `AdmissionBed` (`status = "CLOSED"`, `closedAt = now`) (`:294-298`).
  2. Create new `PatientBill` at `WardType.price`, `status = "VERIFIED"` (`:304-311`); for `INSURANCE` patients resolve `WardTypeInsurancePlan` (active flag **ignored** per Q9 verbatim) via `findByInsurancePlanAndCovered(plan, true)` max-price/exact-match loop — covered → `COVERED`; if top-up difference `> 0` create supplementary `UNPAID "Ward Bed / Room (Top up)"` bill (`PatientServiceImpl.java:1880-1897` pattern reused in accrual).
  3. Create new `AdmissionBed` with `status = "OPENED"`, `openedAt = now` (`:330-334`). **This resets the 24h window.** With a 5-min poll cadence the chained count `N` can be strictly less than `floor(elapsed / 24h)` for long stays.
- Accrual is **idempotent** on same-pass re-entry: if `openedAt` is still < 24h old nothing happens.
- Accrual **stops** when `Admission.status` leaves `{IN-PROCESS, STOPPED}`.
- **Total formula**: `(1 + N) × WardType.price` where `N` = count of completed rolling-24h chained intervals.
- The `@Scheduled` + ShedLock CRON TRIGGER is NOT built (CR-07-Q2, ADR-0018 not ratified).

### Flyway migrations
Inc-07 migrations start at V44 (V43 is the last inc-08 migration `V43__pharmacy_pharmacy_transfer.sql`). 07c DDL adds the `admission_beds` table and `patient_consumable_charts` table (if not already landed in 07a) and any `consumables` masterdata table (if not already in scope). No new `ServiceKind` check constraint changes. Migration numbers V44–V5x reserved for 07a/07b; 07c begins at the next available slot.

### Blocking gates for 07c
1. **Owner confirms Q13 = MEDICINE** (engagement-lead) → unblocks the pricing path (already recommended; see AC-07C-13).
2. **CR-07-Q13-billing-display decided** → before 07c can reproduce legacy `billItem='Medication'` / `description='Consumable: <name>'` literally (ChargeRequest has no such fields; emits `'Medicine'/'Medicine'` today).
3. **ADR-0018 §Context correction issued by solution-architect** → before ratification; gating only the `@Scheduled` cron; the `accrueWardDay` service method + parity oracle proceed now without this gate.
4. **Q2 scheduler owner gate** → only the `accrueWardDay` method + manual trigger are buildable now; the `@Scheduled`/ShedLock binding stays parked.

All four gates are reflected as `frozen: false` on the applicable criteria below.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Given a valid admission in IN-PROCESS status, a nurse uid, a Medicine that has a Consumable masterdata row, and qty > 0, when POST /inpatient/admissions/{uid}/consumables is called, then a PatientConsumableChart is persisted with status hard-coded 'NOT-GIVEN' (not chart.qty-driven, not an enum), the chart.qty equals the request qty, and a PatientBill is created in the caller's transaction.

Given a cash patient (paymentType != INSURANCE and no admission-level insurance plan), when savePatientConsumableChart completes, then PatientBill.status = 'UNPAID', PatientBill.amount = Medicine.price (unit price only, not price*qty), PatientBill.balance = price*qty, PatientBill.paid = 0, and no PatientInvoiceDetail or PatientInvoice is created.

Given an insurance patient with a MedicineInsurancePlan row (covered=true) for the Medicine, when savePatientConsumableChart completes, then PatientBill.status = 'COVERED', PatientBill.amount = PatientBill.paid = planPrice*qty, PatientBill.balance = 0, paymentType = 'INSURANCE', and a PatientInvoiceDetail is appended to a PENDING PatientInvoice (created if none exists); the PatientInvoiceDetail.qty is hard-coded to 1 (not chart.qty) — BUG Q11-1 reproduced verbatim.

Given an admission (cash-on-admission) with no covered MedicineInsurancePlan, when savePatientConsumableChart completes, then PatientBill.status = 'VERIFIED', PatientBill.amount = PatientBill.balance = price*qty, PatientBill.paid = 0, and a PatientInvoiceDetail is appended to a PENDING null-plan PatientInvoice; the PatientInvoiceDetail.qty is hard-coded to 1 (not chart.qty) — BUG Q11-1 reproduced verbatim.

Given a Medicine that has no Consumable masterdata row, when savePatientConsumableChart is called, then the service rejects with BUSINESS_RULE (422) and the verbatim message 'Medicine is not listed as consumable'; no PatientBill or chart row is created.
Given qty = 0 or qty < 0, when savePatientConsumableChart is called, then the service rejects with BUSINESS_RULE (422) and the verbatim message 'Qty can not be zero'; no chart or bill is created.

Given an admission in PENDING status (ward-bed bill not yet paid), when savePatientConsumableChart is called, then the service rejects with BUSINESS_RULE (422) and the verbatim message 'Admission not verified'; no chart or bill is created.

Given an admission in STOPPED, HELD, or SIGNED-OUT status, when savePatientConsumableChart is called, then the service rejects with BUSINESS_RULE (422) and the verbatim message 'Patient already signed off'; no chart or bill is created.

Given no nurse uid supplied (null or missing), when savePatientConsumableChart is called, then the service rejects with BUSINESS_RULE (422) and the verbatim message 'Nurse information not found'; no chart or bill is created.

Given a PatientConsumableChart with status 'NOT-GIVEN', no RECEIVED PatientPaymentDetail, and createdAt within 24 hours, when DELETE /inpatient/consumables/{uid} is called, then the chart, its PatientBill, and its PatientInvoiceDetail (if any) are deleted; NO PatientCreditNote is raised; no stock is restored.

Given a PatientConsumableChart with a RECEIVED PatientPaymentDetail present, when DELETE is called within 24 hours, then a PENDING PatientCreditNote is raised with reference = 'Canceled lab test' (the mislabeled copy-paste from the lab-delete path) — BUG Q11-2 reproduced verbatim. The golden-master asserts creditNote.reference == 'Canceled lab test' as a POSITIVE expectation (code comment + CR-07-Q11 cross-reference required). No stock is restored.

Given a PatientConsumableChart that belongs to a shared PENDING PatientInvoice which also contains at least one sibling PatientInvoiceDetail (e.g. a lab or dressing line on the same invoice), when DELETE is called within 24 hours, then the parent PatientInvoice is deleted UNCONDITIONALLY (reproducing the j=j++ no-op at PatientResource.java:3070-3076 where j is always 0) and all sibling PatientInvoiceDetail rows are cascade-wiped via orphanRemoval=true — BUG Q11-3 reproduced verbatim. The golden-master asserts the sibling details are gone as a POSITIVE expectation (code comment + CR-07-Q11 cross-reference required). This is the most dangerous of the three bugs; fixing it is parked under CR-07-Q11.

BLOCKED (owner confirm Q13 required): Given owner confirms ServiceKind.MEDICINE for consumable pricing, then the consumable charge is priced via PriceLookup.resolve(planUid, ServiceKind.MEDICINE, medicineUid, ccy) and ChargeRequest is built with kind=MEDICINE, serviceUid=Medicine.uid, qty=chart.qty, inpatient=true; no new ServiceKind value or CHECK constraint migration is introduced. Until Q13 is confirmed by engagement-lead this criterion cannot freeze.

BLOCKED (CR-07-Q13-billing-display required): Given the legacy bill line uses billItem='Medication' and description='Consumable: <medicineName>' (PatientServiceImpl.java:2312-2313), then the as-built PatientBill rows produced by 07c reproduce those exact string literals. This criterion is frozen:false because ChargeRequest.java:27-37 has no billItem or description fields and BillingChargeService emits 'Medicine'/'Medicine' today; reproducing the legacy literals requires a billing-API extension via CR-07-Q13-billing-display.

BLOCKED (Q13 confirm required): Golden-master reconciles the cash-UNPAID PatientBill.amount quirk at 2dp: legacy stores amount=Medicine.price (unit price, PatientServiceImpl.java:2308) with balance=price*qty (:2310), while as-built computeAmount(MEDICINE) returns price*qty for amount. A Testcontainers test with a known Medicine.price and qty must assert the resulting PatientBill.amount field value matches the legacy-faithful unit-price value, not price*qty, and the difference is documented as a golden-master note pending Q13 final confirmation. Frozen:false until owner confirms Q13.

Given a PatientConsumableChart with status 'GIVEN', when DELETE is called, then the service rejects with BUSINESS_RULE (422) and the verbatim message 'Consumable already given to patient'; no credit note, no cascade delete, no stock change.

Given a PatientConsumableChart with createdAt more than 24 hours ago, when DELETE is called, then the service rejects (ChronoUnit.HOURS.between(chart.createdAt, now) >= 24 guard); no credit note, no cascade, no stock change.

Given savePatientConsumableChart is called, then NO pharmacy or inventory stock entity (PharmacyMedicineBatch, StoreItemBatch, StockCard, PharmacyMedicine.stock) is read or written in the call; no StockService.decrementFefo or StoreStockService.decrementFefo or PharmacyStockCredit.debitTransferOut is invoked; no InsufficientStockException is thrown. Verify via Testcontainers with a zero-stock seeded Medicine that the call succeeds with UNPAID bill.

Given AuditRecorder (ADR-0007, SHA-256 chained) is wired in the inpatient module, when savePatientConsumableChart completes, then an AuditLog entry is appended for the CREATE action on the PatientConsumableChart entity; the audit log carries actor, timestamp, and entity uid; no PHI field values (patient name, diagnosis) appear in the log payload.
acceptanceCriteria.legacyCite: n/a — modern data-type only (AuditRecorder is net-new per ADR-0007; legacy used manual createdBy/createdOn/createdAt only)

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Ward-accrual parity oracle: Given an Admission in IN-PROCESS status with exactly one OPENED AdmissionBed whose openedAt is T0, when accrueWardDay(admission) is called at T0 + 24h + delta (delta >= 0), then: the old AdmissionBed is closed (status='CLOSED', closedAt=now); a new PatientBill is created at WardType.price with status='VERIFIED'; a new AdmissionBed is created with status='OPENED' and openedAt=now; the admission total ward charge after N chained invocations equals (1+N) x WardType.price.

Ward-accrual de-dup branch: Given an Admission in IN-PROCESS status with two or more OPENED AdmissionBed rows (data anomaly), when accrueWardDay(admission) is called, then ALL opened beds are closed (status='CLOSED', closedAt=now) and NO new PatientBill or AdmissionBed is created that invocation cycle (continue without charge).

Ward-accrual halt on status change: Given an Admission whose status transitions out of {IN-PROCESS, STOPPED} (e.g. to SIGNED-OUT or HELD), when accrueWardDay(admission) is called, then no AdmissionBed is closed, no new bill is created, and the method returns without side-effects.

Ward-accrual no-op branch: Given an Admission in IN-PROCESS with one OPENED AdmissionBed whose openedAt is less than 24 hours ago (ChronoUnit.HOURS.between(openedAt, now) < 24), when accrueWardDay(admission) is called, then no AdmissionBed status changes and no new PatientBill is created.

Ward-accrual insurance path: Given an Admission with an insurance plan where a WardTypeInsurancePlan row exists (covered=true) for the WardType (active flag IGNORED per Q9 — findByInsurancePlanAndCovered(plan, true) only), when accrueWardDay fires and the 24h threshold is met, then the new accrued PatientBill status is 'COVERED' at eligiblePlan.price; if (WardType.price - eligiblePlan.price) > 0 a supplementary UNPAID 'Ward Bed / Room (Top up)' PatientBill is also created at the difference amount.

Ward-accrual chained-window parity oracle (Testcontainers test): Simulate N chained accrual invocations by calling accrueWardDay(admission) N times, each time advancing the test clock by exactly 24h+1min beyond the previous invocation's new openedAt. Assert that after N calls: (a) there are N+1 PatientBill rows total (1 UNPAID admission-time + N VERIFIED accrual bills), (b) the sum of all bill amounts equals (1+N) x WardType.price, and (c) N can be strictly less than floor(totalElapsed/24h) because the poll-latency mechanic resets openedAt on each invocation — document this variance band explicitly in the test. The @Scheduled cron trigger is NOT invoked or configured in this test.

Ward-accrual idempotency: Given accrueWardDay(admission) is called twice in rapid succession (both calls within the same 24h window, second call sees openedAt of the new bed < 24h), then only the first call creates a new bed+bill; the second call is a no-op. Assert total bill count is unchanged after the second call.

BLOCKED (CR-07-Q2 + ADR-0018 ratification required): The @Scheduled cron trigger, ShedLock integration, @EnableScheduling, and the (admission_uid, business_date) ON CONFLICT idempotency key are NOT present in the 07c baseline. No scheduler beans, no shedlock/job_run_log Flyway tables, no cron expression. This criterion documents the ABSENCE as the correct baseline state until the owner ratifies ADR-0018 and authorizes scheduling infrastructure.

Ward-accrual manual trigger: Given the @Scheduled cron is not active, a POST /ops/jobs/ward-accrual endpoint (or equivalent internal test hook) calls accrueWardDay for each qualifying admission; the endpoint requires an authenticated caller (ADR-0006 deny-by-default); no second scheduler-infrastructure dependency is needed for this path.
acceptanceCriteria.legacyCite: n/a — modern data-type only (manual trigger is net-new; legacy used a background Thread)

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Ward-accrual AdmissionBed leak preserved (Q10): At discharge (STOPPED -> SIGNED-OUT), the final OPENED AdmissionBed for the admission is left with status='OPENED' and closedAt=null — it is NOT closed by the discharge path. Only a subsequent accrueWardDay invocation closes it. Assert in a Testcontainers test that after getDischargeSummary the final AdmissionBed row still has status='OPENED'.
acceptanceCriteria.legacyCite: D:/My_Works/HMS/ZANAHMIS-2-feature/Zana-HMIS-API/api/api/src/main/java/com/orbix/api/api/PatientResource.java:5359-5383 (discharge path never touches AdmissionBed); UpdatePatient.java:258-299 (only closer)

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: No stock decrement seam introduced: The inpatient module's 07c slice does NOT publish a new @NamedInterface for consumable stock decrement, does NOT promote StockService.decrementFefo or StoreStockService.decrementFefo to a published cross-module seam, and does NOT reuse PharmacyStockCredit.debitTransferOut for consumables. Compile-time verification: no import of pharmacy.application or inventory.application package-private types from the inpatient module.

PatientConsumableChart status never advances beyond 'NOT-GIVEN' in the 07c baseline: there is no 'give consumable' or status->GIVEN update endpoint or service method. spec.title: Inc-07 (Inpatient & Nursing) — Freezable Build Spec (07a + 07b baseline; 07c partial)

BLOCKED in 07c: the @Scheduled/ShedLock cron trigger (CR-07-Q2 + ADR-0018), the consumable bill-line display literals (CR-07-Q13-billing-display), and the Q13 MEDICINE pricing path (owner-confirm). spec.specBody:
# Inc-07 (Inpatient & Nursing) — Freezable Build Spec

**Charter:** "modern design, exact process." Reproduce VERIFIED legacy `com.orbix.api` behaviour VERBATIM in the frozen baseline. NEVER write a deviation into the baseline — every modern improvement is parked as a named Change Request (CR). Data-model modernization is allowed (double -> BigDecimal `NUMERIC(19,2)` HALF_UP; ULID `uid` + hidden `BIGINT id` per ADR-0014 §1; typed enums mapped 1:1 to the legacy free-text strings via `@Converter`; Flyway). The *process* must be byte-identical to legacy.

**Module state:** `com.otapp.hmis.inpatient` is an EMPTY STUB (`package-info.java` only). Built from scratch.

**Build order (strict):** `prereq seams` -> **07a** (admission + discharge + ward) -> **07b** (nursing charts) -> **07c** (consumable billing-only + ward-accrual oracle).

**Reuse (do NOT rebuild):** clinical (`PatientPrescriptionChart` V27 + `admission_uid` loose ref; `ReferralPlan` V28 with `external_medical_provider_uid` loose ref + `admission_uid`), `billing::api` (`recordClinicalCharge`/`ChargeRequest`, `BillingQueries`, `BillSettledEvent`, `PatientBill.settled`), masterdata (`Ward`/`WardType`/`WardBed`/`WardCategory` V6, `ServiceKind.WARD`/`MEDICINE`/`PROCEDURE`), registration (`PatientType.DECEASED` + `PatientDeceasedEvent`/`PatientClosureListener`), shared kernel (`AuditRecorder` ADR-0007, `shared.documentnumber`, `shared.event`, `ErrorCode`, `GlobalExceptionHandler`), inc-08 pharmacy/inventory stock engine (NOT touched by inpatient — Q12 billing-only).

> **ADR status note (cross-cutting):** ALL ADRs are status `Proposed` (`docs/adr/README.md:3`). No ADR may be relied on to authorize net-new work until ratified. The two owner-gated ratifications here are **ADR-0017** (Q3 lock) and **ADR-0018** (Q2 accrual — and its §Context false premise must be corrected by the solution-architect BEFORE ratification).

> **Audit:** `shared.audit.AuditRecorder` (SHA-256 chained, ADR-0007). NEVER Envers, NEVER device-fingerprint (both confirmed phantom deps). The inpatient surface was UNGATED in legacy; deny-by-default `authenticated()` is approved ADR-0006 hardening (label net-new, NOT a parity assertion). `ADMISSION-CREATE` is the only legacy-valid inpatient privilege code (and it was commented out). Operation suffixes outside `{ACCESS,ALL,CREATE,READ,UPDATE,DELETE,CANCEL,REJECT,VERIFY,APPROVE,ARCHIVE,ALLOW}` are auto-purged — `*-WRITE`/`*-DISCHARGE` are purge-incompatible.

---

## 1. Scope statement

### 1.1 What FREEZES NOW

**Prerequisite seams (lands before any 07a domain code):** the inpatient `@ApplicationModule(allowedDependencies=...)` edge list; the `AdmissionStatus` typed enum (1:1 to legacy strings, no `TRANSFERRED`); a NEW `clinical::api.PrescriptionChartPort` (write owned in clinical, called `inpatient -> clinical::api`); a NEW published admission-scoped billing query for the bills-cleared gate; a NEW `inpatient.application.AdmissionSettlementListener`; and the deceased-event reuse wiring.

**07a — FREEZABLE NOW:** two-phase payment-gated admission lifecycle; admit guards (verbatim 422 messages); consultation->admission activation via the new settlement listener; ward pricing (`WardType.price` cash anchor + active-flag-ignored `WardTypeInsurancePlan` eligibility + the load-bearing top-up split); `AdmissionBed` occupancy/billing ledger; the THREE separate disposition entities (`DischargePlan`, `ReferralPlan`, `DeceasedNote`) with admission AND consultation paths; the hard bills-cleared gate (UNPAID OR VERIFIED block); single-actor approval (`approvedBy = createdBy`) + `AuditRecorder`; deceased terminal via the EXISTING `PatientDeceasedEvent`/`PatientClosureListener`; loose mandatory `external_medical_provider_uid`; open-admission re-admit guard; `@Version`-only bed-claim concurrency (no-double-claim invariant only); `AdmissionStatus` 1:1 (no `TRANSFERRED`); uid surrogate admission numbering; verbatim `AdmissionBed` leak at discharge.

**07b — FREEZABLE NOW:** the FIVE legacy chart entities (`PatientVital` re-use + `PatientNursingChart`, `PatientNursingCarePlan`, `PatientNursingProgressNote`, `PatientDressingChart` new) + the free-text `PatientPrescriptionChart` dosing-note write path + read surface + the 24h DELETE-only guard. NO MAR, NO edit path. The dressing bill AMOUNT/STATUS trichotomy is freezable; the dressing bill-line DISPLAY literals are BLOCKED (see 1.2).

**07c fragments — FREEZABLE NOW:**
- **Consumable billing-only path** (`savePatientConsumableChart` + `deleteConsumableChart`): guards, the 3-way `PatientBill` status outcome (UNPAID/COVERED/VERIFIED), and the THREE Q11 latent bugs reproduced VERBATIM as positive expectations. NO stock touch (Q12).
- **`accrueWardDay` parity oracle**: the idempotent, unbound service method reproducing the rolling-24h total/status semantics, callable via a net-new manual ops trigger.

### 1.2 What is BLOCKED in 07c (does NOT freeze)

| Fragment | Gate |
|---|---|
| The `@Scheduled` + ShedLock + `@EnableScheduling` cron TRIGGER (calendar-night) | CR-07-Q2 + ADR-0018 ratification (after its §Context correction) + scheduling-infra authorization |
| The consumable bill-line literals `billItem='Medication'` / `description='Consumable: <name>'` | CR-07-Q13-billing-display (`ChargeRequest` has no `billItem`/`description` fields) |
| The dressing bill-line literals `billItem='Procedure'` / `description='Dressing: <name>'` | CR-07-Q13-billing-display (SAME display-drift trap; `recordClinicalCharge` derives `billItem=labelFor(kind)`, emitting `'Procedure'/'Procedure'`) |
| The Q13 `ServiceKind.MEDICINE` consumable pricing PATH | OWNER-CONFIRM Q13 = MEDICINE (engagement-lead) — freezable the moment the owner confirms |

**This is the EXACT-PROCESS baseline. It reproduces verified legacy behaviour verbatim and holds every modern improvement behind one of 12 explicit CRs (§4).**

---

## 2. Prerequisite — Cross-module seams (Spring Modulith named interfaces, ports/DTOs, allowed-edge list)

**Overview.** The cross-module wiring that MUST land before any inpatient domain/application code, mirroring the inc-08 `clinical::api`/`billing::api` reuse pattern, so `ApplicationModules.verify()` + ArchUnit stay green (no cycles).

**Allowed-dependency edge list for `inpatient`:** `{ "shared", "clinical :: api", "billing :: api", "masterdata :: lookup", "iam :: lookup" }`. NO reverse edge: `clinical`, `billing`, `registration`, `pharmacy`, `masterdata`, `iam` gain NO dependency on `inpatient`. The edge list deliberately OMITS `pharmacy`/`inventory` (Q12 billing-only — no stock decrement, no FEFO, no last-unit-409).

**Five seams, with decisions:**

1. **`AdmissionStatus` typed enum — OWNED by inpatient, no cross-module leak.** `inpatient.domain.AdmissionStatus` mapped 1:1 to legacy free-text strings via `@Converter` (ADR-0014 §1). Exactly five values: `PENDING("PENDING"), IN_PROCESS("IN-PROCESS"), STOPPED("STOPPED"), HELD("HELD"), SIGNED_OUT("SIGNED-OUT")` — hyphenated DB strings byte-preserved so 3 years of data migrate without value transformation. NO `TRANSFERRED` (Q8). Never crosses a module boundary.

2. **New published clinical seam `clinical::api.PrescriptionChartPort` — WRITE owned in `clinical`, called `inpatient -> clinical::api`.** The chart entity (V27) has REAL intra-module `@ManyToOne` FKs to `Prescription`/`Consultation`/`NonConsultation`, so the write is clinical-owned (mirrors inc-08 `pharmacy` orchestrating dispense while `clinical` owns the `Prescription` flip via `PrescriptionDispensePort`). Inpatient passes `admissionUid` + dosing fields into the port, which writes inside the caller's tx (`Propagation.REQUIRED`, no `@Async`/`REQUIRES_NEW`). **Guard split (confirmed, semantics + verbatim messages MUST be reproduced byte-identically across the boundary):** the admission-IN-PROCESS gate is evaluated in inpatient (it owns `AdmissionStatus`) BEFORE the call; the GIVEN-prescription guard, the exactly-one-encounter CHECK, and the nurse-uid guard are enforced clinical-side. The clinical port exposes uid-only DTOs (`PrescriptionChartView`, `RecordPrescriptionChartCommand`); NO `clinical.domain` entity crosses the boundary (ADR-0008).

3. **Bills-cleared discharge gate — NEW published admission-scoped billing query (IN-BASELINE-REQUIRED for parity).** The legacy gate scans `patientInvoiceRepository.findAllByAdmission(adm) -> PatientInvoiceDetail -> PatientBill.getStatus()` and rejects if ANY bill is `UNPAID` OR `VERIFIED` (`PatientResource.java:5342-5352` discharge / `:5593-5603` referral / `:5851-5882` deceased). `PatientInvoice` is billing-domain-owned; inpatient CANNOT enumerate the bills attached to the admission's invoices by OTHER modules (lab/radiology/procedure/pharmacy). Therefore the gate is reproduced by a NEW published billing seam (e.g. `BillingQueries.admissionHasOutstandingBills(admissionUid)` or `billStatusesForAdmission(admissionUid)`) that performs the `findAllByAdmission` scan INSIDE billing where `PatientInvoice` is owned, mapping legacy String statuses to `billing.domain.BillStatus` 1:1 (UNPAID/VERIFIED block; PAID/COVERED pass — byte-identical predicate). This is NOT optional and NOT a denormalised `billsCleared` flag. NOTE: the existing `BillingQueries.getBillStatus` is documented as scoped to the add-report bill-gate ONLY ("settlement gates MUST NOT use this") and is the WRONG seam for the discharge gate regardless of enumeration concerns.

4. **Payment-driven activation — NEW `inpatient.application.AdmissionSettlementListener` (resolution mechanism explicit).** `ConsultationSettlementListener` (clinical) silently ignores ward/registration bills, so inpatient adds its OWN `@TransactionalEventListener(phase = BEFORE_COMMIT)` on `shared.event.BillSettledEvent`. **Constraint:** `shared.event.BillSettledEvent` is `record(String billUid, Instant settledAt)` only — it carries NO `patientUid` and NO bill-kind, and `SettlementDispatcher` publishes it solely on CASH-bill PAID. The listener therefore resolves the admission by matching the ward-bed `billUid` (the uid persisted on the admission's `AdmissionBed.patientBillUid` at admit) OR via a billing seam resolving `billUid -> patientUid`. The legacy trigger (`PatientBillResource.java:352-365`) is PATIENT-SCOPED on the patient's bill payment (it promotes ALL of the patient's PENDING admissions; it does NOT filter on bill-kind='ward'); reproduce the trigger as legacy does. **Solution-architect escalation:** confirm whether the existing CASH-only `BillSettledEvent` publication even fires for the ward-bed activation case, or whether the event must be extended with `patientUid` (a seam change — NOT a free reuse). The listener imports only `shared.event` (no inpatient->billing reverse edge).

5. **Deceased-finalize publishes the EXISTING `PatientDeceasedEvent` from inpatient's own service (Q6).** The inpatient deceased-finalize service publishes `shared.event.PatientDeceasedEvent(patientUid, actorUsername)` via `ApplicationEventPublisher` in-tx; the existing `registration.PatientClosureListener` flips `Patient.type = DECEASED`. NO new event/listener/field, NO `inpatient -> registration` compile edge. Inpatient does NOT call clinical `ClosureService` (OPD-only).

6. **Consumable pricing seam (Q13) — OWNER-GATED + display-literals CR-blocked.** Reuses `BillingCommands.recordClinicalCharge(ChargeRequest, ctx)` with `kind = ServiceKind.MEDICINE`, `serviceUid = medicineUid`. The MEDICINE pricing PATH is freezable once the owner confirms Q13. The legacy literals require CR-07-Q13-billing-display (`ChargeRequest` has no `billItem`/`description`).

| # | Acceptance criterion | Legacy cite | Frozen |
|---|---|---|---|
| AC-INC07-SEAM-01 | `inpatient` `@ApplicationModule.allowedDependencies` is exactly `{"shared","clinical :: api","billing :: api","masterdata :: lookup","iam :: lookup"}`; `ApplicationModules.verify()` passes with no cycle and no module gains a dependency on inpatient. | n/a (modern boundary; mirrors `clinical/package-info.java:45-47`, `pharmacy/package-info.java:27-34`) | YES |
| AC-INC07-SEAM-02 | `AdmissionStatus` persists 1:1 to legacy strings PENDING/IN-PROCESS/STOPPED/HELD/SIGNED-OUT (hyphens byte-preserved) via `@Converter`; EXACTLY five constants; assert NO `TRANSFERRED`. | `Admission.java:45`; `00-discovery-raw.json` synthesis.groundTruthModel | YES |
| AC-INC07-SEAM-03 | `clinical::api.PrescriptionChartPort` write runs in caller's tx (REQUIRED, no @Async/REQUIRES_NEW); GIVEN-prescription + exactly-one-encounter + nurse-uid guards enforced clinical-side with VERBATIM legacy messages ('Prescription not picked from pharmacy', exactly-one-encounter messages); edge is `inpatient -> clinical::api` only (ArchUnit: no `inpatient -> clinical.domain` reach-through, no `clinical -> inpatient`). | `PatientServiceImpl.java:2544`, `:2549-2562`; `PatientPrescriptionChart.java:57-105` | YES |
| AC-INC07-SEAM-04 | Admission-IN-PROCESS gate evaluated in inpatient BEFORE the clinical::api write; PENDING admission rejects with the VERBATIM legacy 'Could not be done. Admission not verified' (422 BUSINESS_RULE). | `PatientServiceImpl.java:2571-2572` (reject), `:2573-2574` (IN-PROCESS continue) | YES |
| AC-INC07-SEAM-05 | Bills-cleared gate reproduced by a NEW published admission-scoped billing query that performs the `findAllByAdmission -> detail -> PatientBill.getStatus()` scan INSIDE billing and rejects if ANY status is UNPAID OR VERIFIED; statuses mapped to `BillStatus` 1:1 (PAID/COVERED pass). NO live `getBillStatus` iteration from inpatient; NO `billsCleared` field. | `PatientResource.java:5342-5352`, `:5593-5603`, `:5851-5882` | YES |
| AC-INC07-SEAM-06 | NEW `inpatient.application.AdmissionSettlementListener` `@TransactionalEventListener(BEFORE_COMMIT)` on `BillSettledEvent`; resolves the patient's PENDING admission(s) by matching the admission's persisted ward-bed `billUid` (or a `billUid->patientUid` billing seam); on the patient's bill payment promotes PENDING admission(s) -> IN-PROCESS + bed WAITING -> OCCUPIED + signs out IN-PROCESS consultations, atomically. Asserts `ConsultationSettlementListener` does NOT handle ward bills; no inpatient->billing reverse edge. Trigger is patient-scoped on bill payment (NOT bill-kind filtered). | `PatientBillResource.java:352-365`; `ConsultationSettlementListener.java:59-64`; `shared/event/BillSettledEvent.java` | YES |
| AC-INC07-SEAM-07 | Inpatient deceased-finalize publishes the EXISTING `PatientDeceasedEvent(patientUid, actorUsername)`; `PatientClosureListener` flips `Patient.type=DECEASED`. NO new event/listener/field, NO `inpatient->registration` compile edge; does NOT call clinical `ClosureService`. | `PatientResource.java:5851-5882`; `PatientClosureListener.java:84-102` | YES |
| AC-INC07-SEAM-08 | Consumable charge calls `recordClinicalCharge(kind=MEDICINE, serviceUid=medicineUid)` so `PriceLookup.resolve(plan,MEDICINE,medicineUid)` reproduces cash=Medicine.price + MedicineInsurancePlan; no schema change, no new ServiceKind. | `PatientServiceImpl.java:2250-2475`; `ChargeRequest.java:27-37` | **NO — OWNER-CONFIRM Q13** |
| AC-INC07-SEAM-09 | Reproducing the literals `billItem='Medication'`/`description='Consumable: <name>'` is BLOCKED on CR-07-Q13-billing-display (`ChargeRequest` has no such fields); MUST NOT enter the frozen baseline. | `ChargeRequest.java:27-37`; `PatientServiceImpl.java:2312-2313` | **NO — CR-07-Q13-billing-display** |
| AC-INC07-SEAM-10 | New `clinical::api` types (`PrescriptionChartPort`, `PrescriptionChartView`, `RecordPrescriptionChartCommand`) live in `com.otapp.hmis.clinical.api` (inherit `@NamedInterface("api")`), carry uids only (ADR-0014 §1), reuse `shared.domain.TxAuditContext`, expose NO `clinical.domain` entity. | n/a (mirrors `PrescriptionView.java:17-48`, `PrescriptionDispensePort.java:23-39`) | YES |

---

## 3. Section specs + acceptance criteria

### 3.1 — 07a: Two-phase admission lifecycle + admit guards + payment-driven activation

**Overview.** Builds `Admission`, `AdmissionBed`, and the disposition entities from scratch (Flyway V44+). `Admission`: ULID uid + hidden BIGINT id; `status` typed `AdmissionStatus`; loose `patientUid`/`wardBedUid` (no FK; `wardBedUid` DDL `updatable=true` per `Admission.java:62-65` — must NOT be marked non-updatable); `paymentType`/`insurancePlanUid`/`membershipNo`; `admittedAt`/`dischargedAt` (nullable — deceased path never stamps `dischargedAt`); `@Version` from `AuditableEntity`; NO `billsCleared`, NO `businessDayUid`. `AdmissionBed`: per-period occupancy/billing ledger; `status` CHECK `OPENED/CLOSED`; `openedAt` NOT NULL; `closedAt` NULL on terminal admissions (legacy leak, CR-07-Q10); mandatory `@OneToOne PatientBill` (loose `patientBillUid`); loose non-updatable `wardBedUid`/`admissionUid`/`patientUid` (all `updatable=false` per `AdmissionBed.java:40-68`).

**Core rules (verbatim):** `doAdmission` two-phase flow (guard order: 404 patient -> 422 existing PENDING/IN-PROCESS admission -> 404 bed -> 422 bed-not-active -> 422 bed-not-EMPTY; on success bed->WAITING, `Admission(PENDING)`, UNPAID ward `PatientBill`, `AdmissionBed(OPENED)`, `patient.type=INPATIENT`). Payment-driven activation via the seam-06 listener. Charting gate. Worklists as status filters. The three disposition terminals. `@Version`-only concurrency. ADR-0006 deny-by-default RBAC (net-new). uid surrogate numbering. Consultation-block-while-admitted (clinical-owned endpoint — see AC-ADM-13 note).

### 3.2 — 07a: Ward pricing — cash + insurance eligibility + load-bearing top-up split (Q9)

**Overview.** The ward-bed billing engine inside `doAdmission` — the only path that activates the `PENDING -> IN-PROCESS` gate. `WardType.price` is the single flat cash anchor (`NUMERIC(19,2)`; no per-day rate, no per-ward price). Insurance eligibility uses `findByInsurancePlanAndCovered(plan, true)` with the `.active` flag INTENTIONALLY IGNORED (CR-07-Q9 parked). The top-up split is built verbatim but is (near-)unreachable under the legacy query scope; do NOT "fix" the scope. `PriceLookupImpl.java:30-38` explicitly defers the ward referral-override + top-up split — the inpatient service fills this gap directly.

**Activation discriminator (verbatim — corrected):** the line-1880 top-up guard fires when `eligiblePlan.insurancePlan.id != patient.insurancePlan.id AND (WardType.price - eligiblePlan.price) > 0`. The line-1950 `else` (activation at admit: IN-PROCESS/OCCUPIED) is the NEGATION — i.e. when `eligiblePlan IS the patient's own plan OR (WardType.price - eligiblePlan.price) <= 0`. The PRIMARY discriminator is PLAN IDENTITY, NOT price coverage. Because `findByInsurancePlanAndCovered(patient.insurancePlan, true)` already restricts every row to the patient's OWN plan, the plan-id-differs half is (near-)unreachable, the selection loop short-circuits on the first row, and an own-plan covered admission ACTIVATES at admit with NO top-up bill even when `eligiblePlan.price < WardType.price`.

### 3.3 — 07a: Disposition entities + bills-cleared discharge gate + single-actor approval

**Overview.** Three SEPARATE disposition entities (NO typed `kind` discriminator on a shared entity). `DischargePlan` (new `discharge_plans` V44; six nullable narrative TEXT fields; NO required-field validation at create; reuse-existing-PENDING-plan idempotency; first create flips `Admission->STOPPED`). `ReferralPlan` (reuses existing `referral_plans` V28; mandatory loose `external_medical_provider_uid` NOT NULL, no FK; supports inpatient AND outpatient via XOR). `DeceasedNote` (existing `deceased_notes` V28; mandatory `patientSummary`+`causeOfDeath` validated in-service; split user-entered `death_date`/`death_time`; IN-PROCESS->HELD frees bed at HELD; supports inpatient AND outpatient via XOR). Bills-cleared gate shared by all three approve paths (reject if any bill UNPAID OR VERIFIED). Single-actor approval (`approvedBy=createdBy`). The status CHECK and one-plan-per-admission UNIQUE index are NET-NEW DB constraints faithful to the legacy invariant (legacy enforced one-per-admission only via the `@OneToOne` FK + `findByAdmission` reuse) — NOT extracted legacy artefacts.

### 3.4 — 07b: Five nursing-chart entities + free-text dosing-note path (NO MAR)

**Overview.** Adds the nurse-facing charting surface. Five entities (NOT six): `PatientVital` (EXISTS clinical V23 — 07b activates the admission context), `PatientNursingChart`/`PatientNursingCarePlan`/`PatientNursingProgressNote`/`PatientDressingChart` (NEW, V44/V45), plus the `PatientPrescriptionChart` (EXISTS clinical V27) write path. Flat free-text columns; NO FluidBalance/CareActivity sub-entities; NO `NursingProgressNote.kind`; NO CarePlan lifecycle; NO DressingChart `WoundStatus`. DELETE-only (24h window via `ChronoUnit.HOURS.between(createdAt, now) >= 24`); NO edit/update path on any entity. AuditRecorder (NOT Envers).

**Cross-cutting save rules:** require exactly one context; chart/note/care-plan/dressing are admission-only (reject consultation 'Operation not available for outpatients' / nonConsultation 'Operation not available for outsiders'); vitals accept all three contexts; nurse required ('Nurse information not found') for chart/note/care-plan/dressing/prescription; admission gate (PENDING -> 'Could not be done. Admission not verified'; IN-PROCESS -> proceed; else -> 'Could not be done. Patient already signed off').

> **PatientVital staging note (corrected):** legacy `save_patient_vitals` sets status 'PENDING' on create AND update (`PatientResource.java:1088,1107`), and `submit_patient_vitals` is the step that sets SUBMITTED (`:1180-1188`). The as-built inc-05 entity collapsed this two-step into save->SUBMITTED because inc-05 dropped the PENDING state. 07b therefore reproduces the AS-BUILT inc-05 staging entity (which itself carries the pre-existing CR-INC05-VITALS-PENDING gap) — this is NOT a legacy-parity claim.

### 3.5 — 07c: Consumable issue (billing-only) + three latent bugs + ward-accrual parity oracle

**Overview.** Two orthogonal surfaces. (1) `savePatientConsumableChart` (billing-only, Q12 — touches NO stock entity): guard chain, 3-way `PatientBill` status outcome, `deleteConsumableChart` reversal; the THREE Q11 latent bugs reproduced VERBATIM as POSITIVE expectations with mandatory in-code `CR-07-Q11` comment-anchors so no future maintainer "helpfully fixes" them. (2) `accrueWardDay` parity oracle: idempotent, unbound service method reproducing the rolling-24h total/status semantics; callable via a net-new manual ops trigger. NO `@Scheduled`/ShedLock binding (CR-07-Q2).

**Three latent bugs (Q11, verbatim):** (1) `PatientInvoiceDetail.qty` hard-coded to 1 (not chart.qty) in COVERED (`:2372,:2388`) and VERIFIED (`:2441,:2457`) branches; (2) consumable `PatientCreditNote.reference` mislabeled 'Canceled lab test' (`:3053`, copy-paste from lab-delete); (3) the unconditional parent-`PatientInvoice` cascade-delete (`j=j++` no-op at `:3070-3076` -> `j` always 0 -> `if(j==0)` fires unconditionally -> `orphanRemoval=true` at `PatientInvoice.java:80` cascade-wipes sibling details).

**Ward-accrual oracle (`UpdatePatient.java:258-334`):** for admissions in {IN-PROCESS, STOPPED}; de-dup branch (count(OPENED)>1 -> close all, no charge); normal branch (one OPENED + `HOURS.between(openedAt,now)>=24` -> close it, new VERIFIED `PatientBill@WardType.price` with insurance COVERED + top-up split per Q9, new OPENED bed resets the 24h window); idempotent on same-pass re-entry; stops when status leaves {IN-PROCESS,STOPPED}; total = `(1+N) x WardType.price`; with the 5-min poll cadence N can be strictly < `floor(elapsed/24h)`.
